scope: adversarial setup/data-vs-JEPA review: do our synthetic data + distractor diverge from the JEPA corpus in ways that cause the negatives; what data is realistic AND JEPA-aligned
process: 7 dimension experts -> per-finding adversarial verification -> synthesis (37 agents)

findings.title: Our action changes 1 of 301 symbolic slots (mean 1.078); 27.5% of all transitions are byte-identical no-ops — vs V-JEPA's ~85-90%-masked dense targets where every masked patch differs and no copy path exists
findings.our: env/render.py renders a SORTED tree; a command edits one slot. Recomputed on data/v1 val.jsonl (4,800 transitions, scratchpad/symbolic_sparsity.py + signal_vs_haze.py): state-changing transitions change mean 1.078 / median 1 / max 2 of the 301 symbolic slots (258 files + 42 dirs + 1 cwd, per models/gate2.py:43-46). 92.2% of state-changing transitions move exactly 1 slot. Across ALL transitions, 27.5% (1320/4800) are byte-identical in the clean render (all 716 invalid + all 604 valid-no-op; verified the ModernBERT feature diff is EXACTLY 0.0 on every one). Datagen summary.json confirms the mix: 14.99% invalid + 11.97% valid-no-op = ~27% designed-in zero-change.
findings.jepa: JEPA.md:27,44,126: I-JEPA/V-JEPA mask ~85-90% of the input and the target is the full set of masked patch/tubelet embeddings — every masked patch has content and is HIDDEN from the context encoder, so there is no z_t to copy and the multi-block target is spatially/temporally dense (V-JEPA multi-block vs random-tube: 72.9 vs 51.5 K400, masking is load-bearing). PLDM/DINO-WM/V-JEPA2-AC (JEPA.md:85-87): an action/camera motion moves a large fraction of patches, so the next-latent target differs densely from the current one. Their known failure mode (2211.10831, JEPA.md:94) is a distractor SLOWER than the signal, i.e. a dense background that is copyable — NOT a signal that is itself sparse.
findings.causal: Two mechanical consequences drive the copy-collapse in findings 16-r1/17-r1/21/23 and the strong copy floor in finding 18. (a) The gate-2 predictor sees the FULL current state and is identity-at-init (gate2.py:100-101 anchor+zero-delta), so copy is the default and is EXACTLY correct on 27.5% of transitions; the learned predictor can only LOSE there (findings 21/23 report no-op error 1.65-12 vs copy 0 — precisely the 27.5% of transitions where copy is optimal). This is the opposite of V-JEPA, where masked targets are absent from input so copy is unavailable. (b) On the 72.5% of transitions with real change, the change is a needle: a small predictor minimizing summed-slot L2 is dominated by the ~300 slots it should copy, so gradient descent parks it near copy. Finding 18's filtered-random 73-76% plateau and the +30 unreachability are the planning-side echo (needle-shaped improvement, P(needle)~1/865k).
findings.redesign: Raise the state-change density per step so the target is not a needle: (i) enforce a much lower invalid/no-op quota (currently 27%) at datagen (datagen/policies.py); (ii) render only the DELTA or the stdout of the changed region (partial-obs, terminal-jepa.md:26) so the observation is dominated by what changed, not by ~300 unchanged lines; (iii) batch multiple edits per action so mean changed-slots >> 1.
findings.verify_note:
CHANGE-SPARSITY is REAL and correctly measured, but it is a CONTRIBUTING amplifier, not the load-bearing cause of the negatives. Reclassify accordingly.

What holds (verified exactly on data/v1): state-changing transitions move mean 1.078 / median 1 / max 2 of 301 slots (92.2% exactly 1); 27.5% of all transitions (1320/4800 = all no-op + all invalid) are byte-identical in the clean render with ModernBERT feature diff EXACTLY 0.0; state-changing feature copy floor 63.6, global 46.1. gate2.py:100-101 makes copy the identity-at-init default, exactly correct on those 27.5%. The paper contrast is fair for the masking papers (JEPA.md:27,44,126) and mechanism (b) — a needle drowning in ~300 copyable slots under uniform summed-L2 — is directly supported by V-JEPA 2.1's diagnosis (JEPA.md:78) that supervising all-tokens vs only-masked dilutes the signal (they fix it with distance-weighted loss near masks; our loss is the un-fixed version).

Why it is NOT load-bearing for the negatives (the decisive test the finding under-weights): a seen-vs-held-out DISSOCIATION. The SAME genllm-pretrained predictor, same 27.5% no-ops, same 1-of-301 sparsity, BEATS copy on SEEN layouts (train-monitor copy_ratio 0.825 on state-changing; round4 0.852) but LOSES to copy on HELD-OUT layouts (63.95 vs 63.13). gate2.py:363-365 confirms the monitor is training-layout trajectories; the battery is held-out val.jsonl. Sparsity is identical in both regimes, so it cannot be the cause of the held-out failure — layout novelty is. This matches the project's own findings 11-13 (trained encoders decode held-out layouts WORSE than random features) and finding 23's stated conclusion ("the frozen-feature interface is the wall, not the predictor's model family or scale"): the root cause is layout-transfer / representation-identifiability, orthogonal to sparsity.

Corrections to the causal chain:
1. "Collapses toward copy" over-reads: rank-vs-random = 0.833 (copy 0.5), robust across seeds and all 4 predicate kinds — the interface is LIVE. The state-changing failure is that a small net cannot SYNTHESIZE the 768-d contextual target for changed lines (finding 17 round 2's generative-burden diagnosis; VL-JEPA needs ~490M for this) — a mechanism DISTINCT from sparsity, which the finding fuses into one story.
2. Clause (a) "predictor can only LOSE on the 27.5% no-ops" is a minor side effect (no-op haze 1.65 is imperfect-identity of the pretrained trunk, REDUCED by pretraining 5.32→1.65), not the driver of the FAIL verdict; the FAIL is driven by state-changing not beating copy.
3. The planning corroboration (finding 18) is a DIFFERENT sparsity (sparse action-outcome), measured on the ORACLE adapter — independent of the feature copy floor, and finding 19 already re-scoped "+30 unreachable" to a hypothesis and blamed part of the plateau on 7% validity-pool coverage.
4. Paper over-claim to soften: robotics offline data (PLDM/V-JEPA2-AC) DOES contain near-stationary copyable transitions; "no copy path" is only literally true for the masking papers. The defensible divergence is (i) discrete binary change (1 slot full, rest exactly 0, no partial-credit gradient) vs dense-continuous small pixel change, and (ii) an EXACTLY-zero frozen-feature copy path on 27.5%, sharper than any pixel setting.

Actionable implication: since sparsity is not the wall, reducing the no-op rate or up-weighting changed slots alone will NOT fix transfer (finding 21 already showed edit-weight 10 fails by hazing copy slots). The load-bearing lever is what forces a canonical, layout-transferable content code — the untested A2 (grounded temporally-masked prediction) and A3 (state-equivalence / permutation invariance) terms — not a denser target. Change-sparsity is worth listing as a contributing factor (it makes the uniform-L2 metric forgiving of copy and is a genuine modality mismatch vs the masking papers), but it should not be presented as causally sufficient for the held-out negatives.

findings.title: In the frozen ModernBERT target the ONE changed slot carries only 44.8% of the copy-floor L2 — 55.2% is contextual haze spread over ~26 symbolically-UNCHANGED slots, because contextualizing a sorted-line render drifts every line's vector
findings.our: cache-val.pt stores per-slot 768-d ModernBERT vectors of the whole rendered observation. Recomputed (scratchpad/signal_vs_haze.py, 3,480 state-changing transitions): the symbolically-changed slot's vector has norm ~26.5 (median 29.3) and accounts for only 44.8% of the copy-floor L2; the remaining 55.2% comes from a mean of 26.2 (median 26) symbolically-UNCHANGED slots whose 768-d vectors still drift by ~1.0-1.3 each. Per-transition signal/total ratio: mean 0.433, median 0.447. Aggregate haze power / signal power = 1.23. Mechanism verified on a concrete `touch /f/cfg.ini`: exactly 1 new line inserted into a 20-line SORTED tree, 19/20 lines byte-identical, yet ModernBERT's full-attention contextualization moves the vectors of the unchanged lines.
findings.jepa: JEPA.md:7,16,44: the WHOLE POINT of the JEPA target encoder is to DISCARD unpredictable detail so the target is dominated by predictable semantic structure — 'the model spends its capacity on the predictable, semantic structure.' V-JEPA/DINO targets are trained/curated so patch embeddings are stable to nuisance. VL-JEPA (JEPA.md:110) predicts frozen TEXT-SEMANTIC embeddings but with a 490M Llama predictor sized to synthesize them. In none of these does a single unit of true change get diluted below 50% of the target-space distance by an off-the-shelf encoder's context sensitivity.
findings.causal: This is the direct mechanical cause of finding 17's round-1 diagnosis ('the loss pays ~300 slots of diffuse haze over the 1 slot that semantically changed... the changed line's signal, norm ~27, drowns in 301 x 0.2 diffuse error') and the round-2 mirror failure, reproduced with 25x params and pretrained/generative-LLM predictors in findings 21 and 23. A predictor must reduce copy-floor 63 to win; but 55% of that error is high-entropy context drift on unchanged lines that is NOT a function of the action in any low-dimensional way, so a 0.6-316M net can only recover the ~45% signal and lands at copy-or-worse (63.95 in finding 23). Edit-weighting the changed slot (edit-weight=100, gate2.py:411) then destroys no-op calibration (finding 17-r2, 21 ew10) because it stops copying the haze it was previously matching. There is no loss weight that both predicts the signal and copies the haze — exactly what findings 21/23 report across the ew{0,10} sweep.
findings.redesign: Kill the contextual haze at the target: (i) use a per-line/per-slot target that is INVARIANT to sibling reordering — encode each path line in isolation (no cross-line attention) or key targets to path so an unchanged path yields a bit-identical target vector (this is exactly why the symbolic tier-2 pipeline in finding 16 works); (ii) or render one entry per line with fixed absolute slots (a fixed 301-line canvas) so insertion does not shift positions; (iii) or L1/cosine per-slot targets less sensitive to small drift.
findings.verify_note:
CHANGE-SPARSITY / SIGNAL-VS-HAZE — verdict: CONTRIBUTING, not load-bearing (the finding's own "load-bearing" label is downgraded; its causal mechanism is refuted).

REPRODUCED (exact, scratchpad/haze_decomp.py + symbolic_sparsity.py + signal_vs_haze.py, PYTHONHASHSEED=0, 3,480 state-changing transitions, runs/gate2/cache-val.pt): copy floor 63.64; SIGNAL 99,229 = 44.8% vs HAZE 122,225 = 55.2% of copy-floor L2; per-transition signal/total ratio mean 0.433 / median 0.447; ~26.2 unchanged slots drift ~1.0-1.3 each; per-slot SNR 19.8x; `touch /f/cfg.ini` verified (19/20 lines byte-identical). Change-sparsity itself (intuition 1) is REAL and confirmed: 27.5% pure-copy no-ops (copy floor exactly 0), 92.2% of state-changing steps touch exactly 1/301 slots (env/render.py:34 sorted render is the drift mechanism; loss is summed per-slot L2, gate2.py:406-413).

CORRECTIONS to the finding:
1. "aggregate haze power / signal power = 1.23" is a UNITS ERROR. That is the L2-distance ratio (122225/99229). The true squared-L2 power ratio is 0.088. The loss-relevant framing is L2 (55.2% haze), so the qualitative claim stands — but drop the word "power," which overstates energy concentration ~14x.
2. The stated causal link — "a net can only recover the ~45% signal and lands at copy-or-worse (63.95)" — is FALSE and self-contradictory. Counterfactual (scratchpad/counterfactual_predictors.py): recovering signal-only would score 35.1 = 55% of copy = a decisive WIN. The net stays at 63.95 >= copy 63.13 because it recovers NEITHER signal nor haze net-positive on HELD-OUT layouts — not because haze dilutes a recovered signal.
3. DECISIVE evidence the wall is NOT the haze: seen-layout train monitors hit state-changing copy-ratio 0.83-0.85 (runs/gate2/round{4,5}*/train_log.json) while held-out is >=1.0. The haze is deterministic and layout-independent as a mechanism (identical on seen/held-out), so it cannot produce the seen->held-out collapse. The actual cause is layout-entanglement of the learned dynamics — the SAME failure as findings 3/10/13, now at the dynamics level. The ew0->ew10 sweep confirms no signal recovery at any weight (held-out state-changing 66.19->69.09, worse).

WHAT SURVIVES: the haze is a genuine, high-dimensional (top-20 PCs = 68%, shared-mean = 16.5%) capacity tax and a real divergence from the JEPA recipe (JEPA.md:7,16,44: a trained/EMA target encoder discards nuisance; terminal-JEPA uses a frozen off-the-shelf ModernBERT that injects contextual haze into the target). It plausibly makes the fit harder and is consistent with finding 17-r2's "generative burden," and it is a legitimate reason NOT to regress raw frozen features. But it is a CONTRIBUTING amplifier, not the load-bearing cause of the negatives. Recommended reframing: "Frozen off-the-shelf ModernBERT features are non-canonical — a single symbolic edit is diluted to 44.8% of the target-space L2 by deterministic contextual drift over ~26 unchanged lines. This makes feature-space regression a partly-generative target (a real capacity tax, only ~15% paid even on seen layouts) and argues against raw-frozen-feature regression — but the decisive gate-2 failure is layout-entanglement of the dynamics (seen 0.83 vs held-out >=1.0), which haze cannot explain since it is layout-independent." Fix direction implied: predict in a canonical symbolic/decoded space (finding 17-r3's codebook route), not because haze is unbeatable, but because the held-out wall is dynamics generalization, which a symbolic interface sidesteps.

findings.title: Sparsity alone does NOT cause the collapse: the identically-sparse SYMBOLIC target (Oracle, signal-fraction 1.0) beats copy 3.6x and does not collapse — collapse appears only when sparsity meets a CONTEXTUAL-EMBEDDING target
findings.our: Same 1-slot-per-action dynamics, two target spaces. Symbolic (evals/dynamics.py:69-72 OracleAdapter.distance = exact edit count): a state-changing transition has distance 1-2, so signal-fraction is 1.0 by construction, and the tier-2 symbolic predictor (finding 16) reaches state-changing h1 mean 0.297 vs copy 1.076 — 3.6x better, medians 0, 78.7% exact-match. Feature-space (gate2.py:604-605 distance = sum of per-slot 768-d L2): identical sparse dynamics but signal-fraction 0.448, and findings 21/23 land at copy-or-worse. The ONLY difference is whether the target is the symbol or its contextual embedding.
findings.jepa: JEPA.md:16,94: the position paper's bet is that latent targets abstract away unpredictable detail; 2211.10831's failure requires a distractor, not sparsity. So the corpus does not predict that a sparse-but-CLEAN target fails — and our symbolic arm confirms it does not.
findings.causal: This refutes the strong form of the lead's intuition ('sparse target => copy-dominated target => collapse'). Sparsity is necessary but not sufficient: a sparse target with signal-fraction 1.0 (the symbol) is learned near-perfectly. What converts sparsity into collapse is pairing it with an off-the-shelf CONTEXTUAL encoder whose nuisance-sensitivity (finding-2 slow-feature dimension) dilutes the sparse signal below 50% of the target distance. The natural experiment inside the feature space corroborates the SENSITIVITY of the effect to density: 2-slot (mv) transitions have signal-fraction 0.575 vs 1-slot 0.421, and cd (moves only cwd, tiny copy floor) has signal-fraction 0.206 — density and signal-fraction move together. So the correct causal statement is: change-sparsity is load-bearing ONLY through the contextual-embedding target; the frozen-feature interface, not sparsity per se, is the wall (finding 23's own conclusion).
findings.redesign: Two orthogonal fixes, and the data tells you sparsity is the CHEAPER lever than replacing ModernBERT: (A) increase symbolic density per action (fewer no-ops, multi-edit actions, delta-rendering) to push signal-fraction up; (B) more decisively, target a reorder-invariant per-slot representation so signal-fraction returns to ~1.0 as in the symbolic arm. Do NOT expect a dense-but-still-contextual-embedding target to fully fix it — the mv/2-slot result caps the gain at ~0.575.
findings.verify_note:
VERDICT: CONFIRMED (the finding correctly refutes the strong form of the lead's sparsity intuition). Change-sparsity is real and NECESSARY but NOT SUFFICIENT for the collapse; the load-bearing divergence is the frozen CONTEXTUAL-EMBEDDING target, and I reproduced the finding's numbers essentially exactly. Three refinements below.

REPRODUCED (PYTHONHASHSEED=0, using the codebase's own distance functions):
- Symbolic (evals/dynamics.py:69-72): state-changing edit distance mean 1.073 = copy floor 1.076; all transitions 1-2 slots (mv=2 slots, cd/cp/mkdir/rm/touch/write=1); signal-fraction 1.0 by construction.
- Symbolic predictor held-out (runs/tier2/slotpred-v2/battery.json, eval_split="val-heldout-layouts,val-heldout-predicates"): state-changing h1 0.29745 vs copy 1.076 (3.6x), AUC 0.908. GENUINELY HELD-OUT — the "78.7% seen-layout" caveat does not touch this dissociation.
- Feature-space signal-fraction (gate2.py:604-605, clean targets): mean 0.4330, median 0.4472 (=cited 0.448); 1-slot 0.4211, 2-slot 0.5749; cd 0.2059, cp 0.3810, write 0.3820, touch 0.4490, rm 0.4618, mkdir 0.5055, mv 0.5724. All match to 3-4 decimals.
- Feature-space genllm (runs/gate2/round5-genllm-pretrained/battery-clean-hardened.json, 5 seeds): state-changing 63.948 vs copy 63.13 (+0.82, worse), no-op 1.646, invalid 2.634, AUC 0.803<0.9.

CORRECTION 1 (mechanism is two channels, not one): No-op AND invalid clean-target feature distance is EXACTLY 0.0 (n=1320) — clean targets carry zero nuisance on no-ops. So finding-23's no-op/invalid haze is a PREDICTOR SYNTHESIS failure (generative burden, gate2.py:407-410 comment / finding-17 round-2), distinct from the signal-fraction DILUTION that hurts AUC/discrimination. The finding folds both into "nuisance-sensitivity"; they are two mechanisms sharing one root (contextual target).

CORRECTION 2 (the effect is magnitude-inseparability, stronger than "dilution below 50%"): On state-changing, the changed slot moves L2 26.5 while every unchanged-present slot also drifts (mean 1.34), and 51.5% drift >1.0 — comparable per-slot to real edits. The edit-weight remedy (gate2.py:411 threshold >1.0) flags 14.6 slots when only 1.08 truly changed (13.5 false positives), so it cannot isolate the true slot. Correct causal statement: ModernBERT cross-line contextualization injects change on ~13 textually-unchanged lines per edit that is NOT magnitude-separable from signal, corrupting both the plain and the edit-weighted loss.

CORRECTION 3 (downgrade the "dense symbolic target would help" corollary to a hypothesis): There is NO per-verb feature-space error breakdown in finding 23, so "higher signal-fraction likely helps up to a ceiling" is inferred, not measured; and Correction 2 implies the ceiling is set by inseparable bleed, not by the fraction — even dense symbolic changes rendered through a contextual encoder would hit the same wall.

BROADER SUPPORT: finding 22's reconstruction twin (pooled 54.24% vs frozen JEPA pooled 44.4%) shows the JEPA latent>reconstruction premise (JEPA.md:16,61,94) inverts here — the low-bandwidth-text caveat at JEPA.md:61 — independently corroborating "the contextual-embedding interface, not sparsity, is the wall."

findings.title: The per-trajectory banner is a weaker, differently-shaped distractor than JEPA's slow-feature trap, but the STATE CHANGE ITSELF is now the dominant 'dynamic distractor' — reorder-induced context drift plays the role 2211.10831's fixed background plays
findings.our: terminal-jepa.md:27 defines banner (per-traj, ~1 line, ~3% of tokens, 6 bits) + dynamic noise (timestamps/PIDs, resampled per step, render.py:10-12). The fidelity audit (status doc, 'RQ2 caveat') already concedes the banner is 'a far weaker trap than 2211.10831's full-frame distractor.' My measurements add a sharper point: the largest source of unpredictable variance in the CLEAN target is not the banner at all — it is the 55.2% context haze the state change itself injects into unchanged slots (finding above). Findings 14/15a corroborate: dynamic-noise line contaminates every path line by 0.61; banner-swap moves a path line 0.75x as much as a genuine state change moves OTHER unchanged lines.
findings.jepa: 2211.10831 (JEPA.md:94,136): the canonical trap is a distractor SLOWER than the signal — a fixed noise background copied into the representation, satisfying the objective without learning dynamics. Its failing recipes were VICReg/SimCLR, and the distractor was image-scale (full-frame), not a 1-line 6-bit string. The trap is about SLOWNESS relative to signal, and about a distractor that is DENSE enough to dominate the representation.
findings.causal: The banner/dynamic-noise construct is a valid but under-powered analog: it exercises the slow-feature axis (finding 12 shows the slot recipe DID latch the banner at 19x chance, confirming the mechanism exists) but is too low-entropy to be the load-bearing cause of the state-transfer/copy-collapse negatives, which reproduce even with clean targets (findings 13, 17-r1, 21, 23 all use clean targets). The genuinely load-bearing 'distractor' in this domain is the contextual-drift haze that is (a) fast (changes every state-changing step) and (b) unpredictable — the opposite of 2211.10831's slow background, so it does not get copied into a stable code; instead it dilutes the target and starves the predictor. So the banner concept is not ill-conceived but is largely orthogonal to why the world model fails.
findings.redesign: For a faithful 2211.10831 replication, make the distractor DENSE and genuinely slow (a large per-trajectory noise BLOCK, many tokens, static within trajectory) rather than a 1-line banner — that is the shape their trap needs. But recognize the banner is not the project's problem; if the goal is a working agent, spend the effort on the target-representation and density fixes above, not on strengthening the distractor.
findings.verify_note:
The finding is CONFIRMED with two sharpenings and one scoping fix. Recomputed evidence (all absolute paths under /Users/fanzhu/PyCharmProjects/jepa):

BANNER = weak/orthogonal (finding's claim A holds): len(vocab.BANNERS)=64 = exactly 6 bits, 1 of ~19-21 lines (~5%); render.py:10-21. In the clean regime that produces the load-bearing negatives (findings 21/23; gate2.py:283 targets = render_full(st,None,None), verified --input-regime clean), the banner term is identically zero. CLS-trained banner_acc=0.0126≈chance (runs/frozen-modernbert-v1/../sigreg-v1-s0-4k/probe-v2.json) yet CLS-trained still decodes held-out state BELOW untrained (finding 13) — the banner is not necessary for the transfer/copy negatives on the CLS and frozen arms.

CONTEXT-DRIFT HAZE = LOAD-BEARING (finding's claim B, stronger than stated): On val.jsonl, state-changing transitions move mean 1.073 of 301 symbolic slots (0.36%) — extreme sparsity vs dense video/robot targets. Over the frozen CLEAN features (runs/gate2/cache-val.pt), on state-changing transitions 93.6% of total feature-change power (mean per-transition; 96.5% aggregate) is in symbolically-UNCHANGED slots. The copy floor's state-changing h1 = 63.13 (runs/gate2/round4-copyfloor-hardened.json) is entirely this haze; the best learned predictor (round-5 genllm-pretrained, 316M) = 63.95 and cannot beat copy on any of 5 seeds. That IS the copy collapse. The finding's "55.2%" is a scratchpad number matching only the value-only subset (49.7%); the full result is ~93%, so the finding understates its case.

REFINEMENTS: (1) Replace "unpredictable" with "contextually entangled / not slot-locally predictable" — the frozen encoder is deterministic; I measured the SAME line jitters RMS 3.02 across contexts (≈ the 3.91 signal) purely from ModernBERT's bidirectional contextualization and line reordering (88% of state-changing transitions are add/rm/rename). The haze is the layout-entanglement problem (findings 3,13) surfacing at the dynamics-target level, which is why even a generative LLM fails (a generative/context-synthesis burden, not stochastic noise). (2) Scope the "cannot be their cause" claim to CLS/frozen arms: finding-13 encoders trained under regime="both" (slot3 log.json), and the slot arm DID latch the banner 19× (finding 12), so the banner is not orthogonal for the slot arm specifically. (3) The 2211.10831 polarity is opposite (fast-uncopyable vs slow-copied) but both satisfy the objective without dynamics — stated correctly. Net: the banner half is a red-herring for the copy-collapse; the context-drift half is the load-bearing mechanism, so the whole finding earns "load-bearing," not "contributing."

findings.title: A masked file's CONTENT is information-theoretically unpredictable from the rest of the layout — I-JEPA's core precondition (spatial redundancy) is absent by construction
findings.our: datagen/layouts.py:32 draws each file's content class with `rng.randrange(vocab.N_CONTENT)` — i.i.d. uniform over 8 classes, independent of every other file, the path, and the tree shape. Measured on data/v1 (1,626 distinct layouts): content entropy = 2.999/3.0 bits (perfectly uniform); debiased pairwise I(content_f ; content_g within a layout) = +0.00014 bits; I(content_f ; multiset of ALL other contents) debiased against a provably-independent shuffle = +0.010 bits; I(content ; path) debiased = -0.007 bits. A GENUINE-MASKING test (file removed from the render, re-encoded with ModernBERT, predict its class from the pooled remaining lines on held-out val layouts) scored 0.333, but the ground-truth 'predict = mode of the other files' contents' rule scores only 0.133 vs a 0.121 chance floor — i.e. even that 0.333 is the small-layout content-histogram prior, not per-file signal.
findings.jepa: I-JEPA (2301.08243) and V-JEPA (2404.08471) work because a masked image block / spatio-temporal tubelet is HIGHLY predictable from visible neighbors: natural images/video have strong local spatial+temporal redundancy (a masked patch of grass is inferable from surrounding grass). JEPA.md §3 states the whole point is to 'spend capacity on the predictable, semantic structure of the world'; §9.2 makes masking design load-bearing precisely because there IS structure to exploit (V-JEPA multi-block 72.9 vs random-tube 51.5 on K400). The predictor learns p(target | context) and that conditional is far from uniform.
findings.causal: The deprioritized A2 masking arm ('mask a subtree, predict from visible context') is not merely 'toothless' as the status doc says — for content it is provably ill-posed: p(masked content | visible layout) = the uniform prior, so the Bayes-optimal predictor IS the marginal and prediction error cannot be reduced below the entropy floor no matter the model. Any masked-prediction objective over content therefore supplies ZERO learning signal that could force a transferable content code into latents; the encoder can satisfy it by emitting the content marginal. This is a distinct, deeper failure than the forward-dynamics negatives (findings 11-13, 21-23): those show the recipe doesn't transfer; this shows one candidate fix (A2 spatial masking) is dead on arrival for the content feature that RQ1's probe targets.
findings.redesign: If a masking arm is pursued, make content CORRELATED so masking has signal: e.g. content determined by a rule (content = hash(path) mod 8, or content tied to sibling/parent, or a small latent 'project type' per layout that dictates a joint content distribution). Then I(masked content ; visible) > 0 and a transferable code is BOTH necessary and sufficient to win. Alternatively drop content prediction from the masking objective entirely and mask only what has real MI (see next finding: directory/existence structure).
findings.verify_note: CONFIRMED and load-bearing for the A2-spatial go/no-go, with one scope clarification. Reproduced numbers (data/v1, 1,626 distinct layouts, 19,549 file instances): content entropy 2.9999/3.0 bits; raw pairwise plug-in MI 0.000140 bits -> debiased -0.00003; debiased I(content;others) +0.0069; debiased I(content;path) -0.0018. Mode-of-others accuracy 0.128-0.145 vs 0.125 uniform / 0.127-0.135 empirical majority floor = chance within 95% CI. Mechanism: p(masked content | visible co-temporal layout) equals the uniform marginal, so a spatial masked-prediction objective over content (original A2) supplies zero signal below the 3-bit entropy floor no matter the model — I-JEPA/V-JEPA's spatial/temporal-redundancy precondition (JEPA.md §3, §9.2; 2301.08243, 2404.08471) is absent by construction at datagen/layouts.py:32 (env/vocab.py:6). Code anchors verified. Two scoping caveats the finding already respects and that must be preserved when citing it: (a) this kills the ORIGINAL co-temporal spatial A2 for CONTENT only; the REVISED temporal A2 (predict masked obs_t from obs_{t-1}+action) is well-posed — measured temporal content persistence is 99.68% — and remains a live candidate. Existence/cwd probe targets are also not covered by this content-only kill. (b) This does NOT causally explain findings 11-23 (all forward-dynamics/probe arms; models/gate2.py, evals/dynamics.py; none ran spatial A2) — it forecloses a candidate remedy before it runs. So 'load-bearing' = decisive for the A2-spatial decision and a genuine sharpening of the status doc's 'toothless' (terminal-jepa-status.md:32) from heuristic to information-theoretic, NOT 'a cause of the observed collapse-to-copy.' The finding states this distinction itself, so it is confirmed as written; only a careless reader conflating the two senses would err.

findings.title: Existence DOES carry learnable redundancy (the parent-directory constraint), but it is fully visible in the observation text, so masking it teaches nothing new either
findings.our: env/actions.py enforces (and datagen respects) the invariant that a file at parent P can exist only if P is a directory that exists (touch/write/cp require `f[:-1] in state.dirs`, e.g. _apply_touch actions.py:119). Measured on data/v1: I(parent_exists ; file_exists) = 0.115 bits — when a parent dir is absent, file-present rate is EXACTLY 0.0000 (335,442 slots); when present, 0.2325. Within valid (parent-present) slots, sibling existence is nearly independent: I(n_siblings_present ; this_file_present | parent exists) = 0.019 bits (a weak effect from the n_files~U(4,20) layout-size prior, layouts.py:24). Content-given-existence, however, remains 100% linearly decodable FROM ITS OWN LINE (finding 14 replicated: 1.000 held-out).
findings.jepa: In I-JEPA the redundancy a masked patch exploits is NOT already spelled out elsewhere in the visible input in symbolic form — you must synthesize the abstract content. The hierarchical/interaction structure that makes masking productive (Causal-JEPA 2602.11389 masking whole object slots to force interaction reasoning) requires that the hidden part be inferable ONLY by modeling relations, not by reading a co-present token.
findings.causal: The domain's one genuine redundancy (tree structure) is not hidden: a rendered line '/a/b/x.txt [c3]' already textually asserts that dirs /a and /a/b exist (the path prefix). So masking a directory and predicting its existence from the visible file lines is inferable by a trivial string rule, not by learning an abstract world model — exactly why A2's original spatial-masking form is toothless HERE while it is load-bearing for images. This refines the lead's intuition #1: it is not only that changes are sparse; it is that the domain's inter-slot structure is either zero-MI (content) or already-explicit (existence), leaving no hidden-but-inferable target for masking to reward. Contributing (not load-bearing) because A2 was already deprioritized; this quantifies WHY the deprioritization was correct and closes the 'maybe masking would have worked' door.
findings.redesign: The status doc's SURVIVING A2 form (predict masked region of obs_t from obs_{t-1}+action) is the right pivot because it swaps the zero-MI spatial axis for the TEMPORAL axis where MI is high (obs_{t-1}+action fully determines obs_t). Make that the arm, and give it grounded frozen/clean targets so the encoder can't re-drift to distractors. Do NOT invest in same-timestep subtree masking.
findings.verify_note:
FINDING (contributing — confirmed as a diagnosis-completeness result, with two refinements): Existence carries learnable redundancy (the parent-directory invariant, actions.py:119), but that redundancy is fully VISIBLE — and more precisely SELF-WITNESSING — in the observation, so masking it teaches nothing an abstract world model must synthesize.

RECOMPUTED (data/v1 val.jsonl, PYTHONHASHSEED=0): I(parent_exists;file_exists)=0.1164 bits (claim 0.115 ✓); file-present rate EXACTLY 0.000000 when parent absent (✓); ≈0.21 when present (claim 0.2325 — close; exact 335,442-slot denominator did NOT reproduce, I get 1.89M full / 1.76M depth-2, cosmetic scope difference). Within valid slots siblings are near-independent: I(siblings;file|parent exists)=0.025 bits (claim 0.019 ✓ same order). Content is ~uniform i.i.d. (H=2.985/3.0 bits, layouts.py:32) ⇒ zero exploitable cross-slot content MI. Content-given-existence decodes 1.000 held-out (codebook.pt, finding 14 replicated).

REFINEMENT 1 (the finding UNDER-states its own case): The "trivial prefix rule over visible file lines" is only 95.36% accurate — it fails on EMPTY directories (4.64% of dir slots, no child witness). The load-bearing mechanism is stronger and is already in the code: frozen_probe.py:11-12 — existence is STRUCTURAL under the path-keyed readout (a slot is present iff its own line is rendered iff the path exists), so existence is asserted on the slot's OWN line and masking it is toothless for empty dirs too, no prefix rule needed. render.py:30-34 renders every dir its own line.

REFINEMENT 2 (against the lead's intuition #1 as stated, orthogonal to this finding): symbolic edits ARE sparse (median 1 existence-flip/transition, cache-val), but the FEATURE-space target is NOT ~copy — median 11 of ~26 present slots move (median 1 true edit + median 10 contextual-drift slots ModernBERT re-encodes), so the trained predictor's problem is a 1-slot signal drowning in ~10 slots of contextual haze (battery AUC 0.647), not a pure 1/301 copy target.

CAUSAL STATUS: CONTRIBUTING, not load-bearing. This quantitatively closes the "maybe I-JEPA/Causal-JEPA-style spatial masking (A2) would have worked here" door — correctly, and consistent with the status doc having already deprioritized A2 (plan §A2 line 32) on the same qualitative grounds and citing Causal-JEPA (JEPA.md:90) as the mechanism it lacks. But the OBSERVED negatives come from non-masking arms and are caused by the frozen-feature interface / generative burden, not by the absence of a maskable target: finding 23's pretrained generative SmolLM2-360M over frozen features STILL fails the gate (state-chg 63.95 vs copy 63.13, no-op 1.65≠0, AUC 0.803<0.9, collapses toward copy on held-out layouts), and the status doc concludes "the frozen-feature interface is the wall, not the predictor's model family or scale." Fits JEPA.md:61's low-bandwidth-text caveat (reconstruction is fine for discrete/low-bandwidth text — cf. finding 22's recon twin) and JEPA.md:94's slow-feature trap (the actual load-bearing distractor mechanism, distinct from this masking-redundancy dimension).

findings.title: The forward-dynamics target is NOT ~copy: exactly 1 symbolic slot changes per step, but it is a HIGH-MI, fully-determined target — the negatives are a representation-interface wall, not a starved-signal wall
findings.our: Forward dynamics (gate 2, models/gate2.py) predicts next-state features from current+action — not masking. Measured on data/v1: on state-changing transitions the SYMBOLIC change is exactly 1 slot (median 1, mean 1.07 of 301; distribution {1:6447, 2:510}); the action fully determines which slot and its new value (deterministic env, env/actions.py). So p(next_state | state, action) is a delta function — the OPPOSITE of the content-masking case: MI(target ; input+action) is maximal, not zero. In feature space the change spreads to ~14 of 301 slots (median 14, mean 14.6) purely via ModernBERT bidirectional cross-attention bleed — the changed line's edit perturbs ~13 textually-unchanged neighbor line-vectors (this IS finding 15a's 79%-nuisance and round-1's diffuse-haze failure).
findings.jepa: The lead's intuition #1 predicts the target is '~copy' and 'starves the dynamics signal', analogous to 2211.10831's slow-feature trap where a copy satisfies the objective. But V-JEPA-2-AC (2506.09985) and PLDM (2502.14819) also predict mostly-persistent scenes (most of a robot frame is static between steps); their action-conditioned predictor still learns because the action determines the small changed region and that mapping has high MI. Sparsity of change is NOT the same as low MI of the target.
findings.causal: This REFUTES the 'prediction target is ~copy → starves dynamics signal' framing for the forward-dynamics arm. The signal is present and maximal-MI: finding 1/47's own trained CLS predictor beats copy 6.7x on state-changing, and gate-2 slotpred hits 78.7% exact-match on seen layouts — the dynamics ARE learnable when representation isn't the problem (finding 16). The negatives (findings 21-23: genllm still collapses toward copy on HELD-OUT layouts, AUC 0.803) are LAYOUT-ENTANGLEMENT of the representation, not signal starvation: the model can't identify WHICH abstract slot the action names on an unseen arrangement, because the frozen per-line features are content-complete but not path-canonical (finding 14/22). So the copy-collapse is a symptom of a missing canonical coordinate system (A3's target), not of a copy-shaped target.
findings.redesign: Since the wall is per-line non-canonicality (not target sparsity), the highest-value lever is A3 state-equivalence invariance (path-keyed equivariance + order-invariant aggregation) to manufacture canonical coordinates, composed with the surviving temporal-A2 arm. A copy-resistant harder target won't help; a representation that maps the same abstract path to the same code across layouts will.
findings.verify_note:
CONFIRMED (with evidence-line corrections). The forward-dynamics target is high-MI and fully determined, NOT ~copy — this refutes intuition #1's 'sparse change → copy target → starved signal' framing for the gate-2 arm, and the held-out negatives are a representation-interface (path-canonicality) wall, not signal starvation.

Independently re-derived on data/v1 (PYTHONHASHSEED=0):
- Symbolic change per state-changing transition: median 1, mean 1.073, dist {1:6447, 2:510} (exact). state-changing = 72.5% of val.
- Determinism/MI (my check): the action's path arg exactly names the changed slot in 6472/6472 non-cd state changes (100%); cd → cwd slot. env/actions.py apply() is a pure deterministic function, so p(next|state,action) is a delta — MI is maximal, the OPPOSITE of content-masking. (Refutes intuition #1.)
- Feature-space changed slots: median 14, mean 14.60 (exact) — ~13-14 unchanged neighbor lines move via ModernBERT bidirectional cross-attention.
- Copy floor 63.13 / AUC 0.5 / VoE 0.514; genllm-pretrained held-out (5 seeds) state-chg 63.95 (+0.82 above copy, does NOT beat it), no-op 1.65, invalid 2.63, AUC 0.803<0.9, VoE 0.525≈copy — copy-collapse confirmed (finding 23).

The load-bearing dissociation: same predictor SHAPE, oracle symbolic representation → gate cleared (tier-2 slotpred held-out AUC 0.906, state-chg 0.277); frozen ModernBERT features → copy-collapse (AUC 0.803). Target MI identical; only the representation changed → the representation interface is the wall. finding 16's 78.7% is a seen-layout train-monitor number, not held-out.

Independent positive test of the 'not path-canonical' mechanism (my own, on cache-val.pt): a fixed (path, content class) drifts median 3.12 across different layouts vs a content-flip signal of median 4.03 → nuisance/signal 0.77. Frozen per-line features are content-complete but layout-entangled, so a predictor trained on seen layouts cannot resolve slot identity/value on unseen arrangements. This is the A3-shaped 'missing canonical coordinate system' the finding names.

Paper grounding is correct: V-JEPA-2-AC (2506.09985) / PLDM (2502.14819) also predict mostly-persistent scenes and learn because the action determines the small changed region (high MI); 2211.10831's slow-feature COPY trap does NOT apply here (target is high-MI; copy is provably suboptimal on the copy-resistant alt-action foil, VoE≈0.5).

CORRECTIONS to the finding's evidence lines (do not change the verdict):
1. The parenthetical '(this IS finding 15a's 79%-nuisance ...)' conflates two distinct quantities. finding 15a's 79% = 15·0.607²/2.65² = 0.786 is DYNAMIC-NOISE-LINE contamination on a content-flip signal (justifies clean targets; the intuition-#2 distractor). The ~14-slot feature spread is state-change SPILLOVER, whose power is ≈15% of signal (my recompute), not 79%. Same root cause (bidirectional bleed), different measurements — decouple them in the writeup.
2. State the isolation source explicitly: genllm ran at edit-weight 0 ONLY, so its no-op/invalid haze (AUC<0.9) is partly a uniform-loss diffuse-haze artifact. The clean 'loss-weighting does not rescue it' evidence comes from ROUND-4's {0,10}×{pretrained,randinit} sweep (best AUC 0.879<0.9; ew10 worsens haze and pushes state-chg above copy), not round-5. genllm ew10 remains inferred, not measured.
3. finding-47's 6.7x predictor is itself miscalibrated (40.6x WORSE than copy on no-ops) — it evidences 'dynamics learnable when target is high-MI' but is not a gate-clearing counterexample; state it as such.
4. Carry finding 23's caveats: single TRAINING seed; SmolLM2-360M < Llama-3.2-1B (scale not exhausted); not a full VL-JEPA port (fixed slot SET, same-encoder targets).

On intuition #2: the banner (per-trajectory static) is a valid 2211.10831 slow-feature analog per terminal-jepa.md §3, and noise is a fast per-step distractor — but both are ORTHOGONAL to the forward-dynamics negatives, because gate 2 trains on CLEAN targets (banner=None/noise=None) and strips the distractor from the prediction target by construction. The distractor is an encoder-side/Track-B concern (findings 12-13), not the gate-2 copy-collapse cause.

Relevant files (absolute): /Users/fanzhu/PyCharmProjects/jepa/terminal-jepa/models/gate2.py (predictor shapes, gate2.py:411 changed-slot criterion, ew0 default); /Users/fanzhu/PyCharmProjects/jepa/terminal-jepa/env/actions.py (deterministic pure transition, _finish ttype); /Users/fanzhu/PyCharmProjects/jepa/terminal-jepa/evals/dynamics.py (battery, copy floor, VoE); /Users/fanzhu/PyCharmProjects/jepa/terminal-jepa/probes/target_noise.py (the 79% is noise-line, not spillover); /Users/fanzhu/PyCharmProjects/jepa/terminal-jepa/runs/gate2/round5-genllm-pretrained/battery-clean-hardened.json (AUC 0.803); /Users/fanzhu/PyCharmProjects/jepa/terminal-jepa/runs/gate2/round4-copyfloor-hardened.json (63.13); /Users/fanzhu/PyCharmProjects/jepa/terminal-jepa/runs/tier2/slotpred-v2/battery-hardened.json (oracle-rep gate cleared, AUC 0.906); /Users/fanzhu/PyCharmProjects/jepa/terminal-jepa/runs/sigreg-v1-s0-4k/predictor-vs-copy.json (6.7x, and 40.6x worse on no-op); /Users/fanzhu/PyCharmProjects/jepa/terminal-jepa/terminal-jepa.md §3 line 27 (distractor rationale).

findings.title: The banner distractor is a VALID but far-too-weak analog of 2211.10831's slow-feature trap; the true nuisance is the encoder's own bidirectional bleed, which the papers do not face
findings.our: env/render.py:15-21 injects a per-trajectory banner (one line, 64-way, ~6 bits, env/vocab.py:29-32) and per-step dynamic noise (_noise_line, timestamps/PIDs). The banner is static within a trajectory, varies across trajectories — a textbook slow distractor. But it is ~3% of tokens (status audit RQ2 caveat) vs 2211.10831's full-frame noise. Separately measured: one symbolic edit contaminates ~14 of 301 frozen feature slots (median 14) via ModernBERT bidirectional attention — an in-band nuisance that is 79% of signal power (finding 15a) and is NOT a designed distractor at all.
findings.jepa: 2211.10831's trap requires the distractor to be SLOWER than the signal and to occupy enough bandwidth that copying it satisfies the variance/prediction objective (their failing recipes were VICReg/SimCLR on full-frame noise). JEPA.md §5/§10 flag this as having 'no general solution beyond data diversity'. The distractor must be comparable in bandwidth to the signal to actually trap the objective.
findings.causal: Partially refines the lead's intuition #2. The banner IS a conceptually valid analog (the untrained encoder latches it at 5-7x chance, and the slot recipe captures it at 19x chance — finding 12 — reproducing 2211.10831's prediction), so it is not 'ill-conceived'. But it is too low-bandwidth to be load-bearing for the negatives: the CLS recipe suppresses it (banner-swap ratio 0.075 << 0.5 red line, finding 2/13), so it is NOT what breaks transfer. The genuinely damaging nuisance is emergent, not designed: bidirectional-attention bleed spreads a 1-slot symbolic edit across ~14 feature slots, forcing the from-scratch feature-space predictors to synthesize 768-d contextual embeddings for lines that didn't change (finding 17 rounds 1-2, finding 21). The papers' encoders are trained end-to-end so their targets don't have this frozen-encoder cross-contamination.
findings.redesign: Two independent moves: (1) if 2211.10831 replication is a goal, raise banner bandwidth to signal-comparable (multi-line, high-entropy, or a full fake-directory distractor subtree) — the queued higher-entropy knob. (2) For the actual transfer wall, prefer causal-over-set or explicitly path-masked attention so a slot's target feature does not bleed into neighbors, OR use clean per-line targets with a changed-slot-only loss (already the edit-weight knob) so the predictor isn't taxed by synthesizing 13 unchanged contextual vectors per step.
findings.verify_note:
REFINED FINDING (red-herring on the bleed thesis; the banner half stands): The banner distractor is a VALID but far-too-weak analog of 2211.10831's slow-feature trap (6.0 bits, 4.7% of tokens; env/vocab.py:29-31, env/render.py:15-21), and it is correctly NOT load-bearing for the negatives (CLS-trained banner-swap ratio 0.075 in finding 2; frozen path-keyed slot-space ratio 0.371 measured here, still under the 0.5 red line; and gate-2 targets render clean so the banner never enters the prediction target). That refines lead intuition #2 correctly: not ill-conceived, just too low-bandwidth to trap.

BUT the finding's core claim — that the encoder's bidirectional bleed is "the genuinely damaging nuisance the papers do not face" — is a RED HERRING. Recomputed from data/v1 + runs/gate2/cache-val.pt (clean regime, the actual gate-2 target space):
- A 1-slot symbolic edit (median 1, mean 1.078) does spread to median 14 feature slots at dz>1.0 — the bleed is real.
- But it carries only ~8% of change power (median; 13% mean), NOT the "79%" the finding cites. The 79% is finding-15a's DIRTY-input dynamic-noise contamination, a different mechanism stripped from clean targets by construction — the finding misattributes it.
- The bleed is DETERMINISTIC and learnable: no-op and invalid transitions produce exactly zero feature change on all 301 slots, so features are a deterministic function of (state, action). This fails 2211.10831's definition of a trap (which needs an uncontrolled distractor SLOWER than the signal; the bleed is perfectly edit-correlated).
- The "papers train end-to-end so no frozen-encoder cross-contamination" premise is false: V-JEPA 2-AC (frozen ViT-g, JEPA.md:86), DINO-WM (frozen DINOv2, JEPA.md:87), and VL-JEPA (frozen V-JEPA 2 + frozen text-encoder targets, JEPA.md:110) all predict over frozen encoders with the same bidirectional/spatial ViT-patch bleed — and succeed. Bidirectional bleed is the norm, not a terminal-JEPA-specific wall.

THE ACTUAL LOAD-BEARING DIVERGENCE is layout transfer / absolute-state non-identifiability, not the bleed. Dispositive dissociation: round5 genllm seen-layout state-changing copy_ratio = 0.83 (predictor beats copy, models the bleed fine) vs held-out 63.95/63.13 ≈ 1.01 (worse than copy). The bleed is present in both regimes; only held-out fails. Modeling only the ~1 edited slot removes ~72% of copy error, and the arms cannot even do that on unseen layouts — they die at the transfer wall before the ~28% diffuse-bleed residual matters. The bleed is at most a second-order modeling burden ("contributing"), not the cause of the negatives. Downgrade this finding from "contributing" to "red-herring" as to the bleed mechanism; keep the banner-is-too-weak sub-claim as confirmed. Relevant files: /Users/fanzhu/PyCharmProjects/jepa/terminal-jepa/models/gate2.py (targets clean, threshold 1.0), /Users/fanzhu/PyCharmProjects/jepa/terminal-jepa/env/render.py, /Users/fanzhu/PyCharmProjects/jepa/terminal-jepa/env/vocab.py, /Users/fanzhu/PyCharmProjects/jepa/JEPA.md (lines 86/87/110/94/136), /Users/fanzhu/PyCharmProjects/jepa/terminal-jepa-status.md (findings 15a, 21, 23), /Users/fanzhu/PyCharmProjects/jepa/terminal-jepa/runs/gate2/round5-genllm-pretrained/{train_log.json,battery-clean-hardened.json}, /Users/fanzhu/PyCharmProjects/jepa/terminal-jepa/runs/gate2/cache-val.pt.

findings.title: Consecutive latent velocities are a near-orthogonal random walk (cos ~0), the exact opposite of the smooth/straight trajectories temporal-straightening and PLDM gradient-planning assume
findings.our: Recomputed on the frozen ModernBERT val cache (runs/gate2/cache-val.pt, 200 held-out-layout trajectories, 24 steps each). For consecutive velocity vectors v_t = z_{t+1}-z_t in feature space, cos(v_t, v_{t+1}) has mean -0.028 / median -0.012 over the full dense 301x768 latent (n=2459 nonzero pairs), and +0.014 / +0.037 in a pooled-768 CLS-like latent. Restricted to consecutive state-changing steps only: dense cos mean -0.028 (77.2% of pairs have cos<0), pooled cos mean +0.014. Essentially 0% of consecutive velocities align (cos>0.5: 0.000 dense / 0.026 pooled). Step magnitudes are bimodal: 27.5% of steps are exactly zero-velocity (no-op/invalid, byte-identical target) and the rest jump a near-constant ~30.9 median L2 — a hard on/off, not a smooth flow.
findings.jepa: Temporal Straightening (2603.12231) adds a regularizer forcing consecutive velocity vectors to ALIGN (it is provably a better-conditioned planning objective) and reports gradient-based planning gains e.g. 44->94% on PointMaze precisely because robot/video latent trajectories are locally straight (cos(v_t,v_{t+1})->+1). PLDM (2502.14819) plans with MPPI over smooth latent trajectories; JEPA.md §5/§9(4) names trajectory-straightening as the residual fix that unlocks cheap ~10x-faster gradient planning. All assume a continuous, slowly-turning latent path where the local gradient of latent-distance-to-goal points toward the goal.
findings.causal: The project's planning negatives (finding 18: unfiltered CEM +4 over random, well below the +30 RQ3 bar; 'distribution-sharpening has nothing to grip when improvement is needle-shaped') are the direct symptom of this. A latent that random-walks (cos~0) each command has no smooth cost gradient to descend: temporal-straightening/gradient-planning are inapplicable, and even sampling planners get no distributional signal because neighboring action choices land at near-orthogonal, uncorrelated latent points. It also explains why per-step latent distance carries no compositional gradient toward a two-arg needle (finding 18's file_exists_with_class failure). This is a continuity-inductive-bias mismatch, not a tuning problem.
findings.redesign: Do not rely on any smoothness-exploiting mechanism (temporal-straightening, gradient planning, PLDM-style MPPI over smooth latents) in this domain, and do not treat their absence as a bug to fix. For planning, either (a) plan in the discrete symbolic space where 'distance' is edit-count with a real compositional gradient (the oracle/tier-2 path), or (b) shape the latent cost with an IQL value function (2601.00844) so Euclidean distance encodes reachability rather than raw geometry. Alternatively, lengthen the effective step by making single commands move a correlated block of state (see finding on density) so consecutive velocities acquire correlation.
findings.verify_note:
DESCRIPTIVELY TRUE, CAUSALLY A RED HERRING. The dense-latent cos~0 numbers reproduce exactly (mean -0.0278, median -0.0124, 77.2% cos<0, 0% aligned; 27.5% zero-velocity; nonzero L2 median 30.88), so "consecutive latent velocities are near-orthogonal, unlike the straight trajectories continuous-control JEPA assumes" is a real observed divergence. But it is not load-bearing for the negatives, for four reasons grounded in the project's own artifacts:

1. cos~0 is the GENERIC geometry of a discrete state space, not a pathology of our data or the ModernBERT features. In the clean context-free ground-truth symbolic one-hot latent (perfect world model), consecutive-velocity cos is still mean -0.011, median EXACTLY 0.000, 0% aligned. Discrete transitions to disjoint state coordinates are orthogonal by construction. The straight/cos->+1 trajectories of PointMaze/PLDM are a property of continuous kinematic manifolds (position integrates velocity), absent by definition in a symbolic terminal — this is the continuous-vs-discrete divergence, correctly noted but mislabeled a "random walk pathology."

2. The claimed causal link to the planning negatives is REFUTED by runs/tier1/cem-oracle.json: on the oracle representation (same cos~0 geometry) the sampling CEM filtered planner reaches 73% (random-filtered 76%). Sampling planning works fine in the cos~0 latent; the finding's "even sampling planners get no distributional signal" is false.

3. Temporal-straightening (2603.12231) is, per JEPA.md §5/§9(4), a residual conditioning fix that lets GRADIENT planning replace CEM (~10x faster); it is not a prerequisite for planning. The actual named planning bottleneck is the COST landscape (2601.00844). finding 18/19 pin the +4-over-random / needle result on P(needle)~=1/865k factored-action sparsity in SYMBOLIC edit-distance (wrong-class write doesn't move distance) — and finding 19 shows goal-distance ranking = 0.99 even for file_exists_with_class, so the gradient DOES point at the needle. The finding misattributes a sparse-reward/combinatorics failure to latent velocity geometry.

4. The "cross-checked in two parameterizations" robustness claim is fragile: the pooled +0.014 reproduces only under mean-over-present-slots; the equally-standard mean-over-all-301-slots gives cos +0.33 / median +0.60 / 56% ALIGNED (opposite verdict), a ModernBERT-anisotropy artifact (0.93 mean pairwise line-vector cosine). So the pooled check doesn't corroborate — it shows normalization-dependence.

The genuinely load-bearing fact adjacent to this finding is the target-density mismatch it obscures: a command changes 1 SYMBOLIC slot but 27 FEATURE slots (27:1 contextualization-ripple noise-to-signal in the prediction target). That copy-starvation mechanism (intuition 1 / finding 22) — not velocity-straightening — is what causes the copy-collapse and dynamics-gate failures. RECOMMENDATION: demote this finding from load-bearing to red-herring; fold its one true kernel (discrete state -> no smooth latent flow -> cheap gradient planning is inapplicable) into the cost-landscape/sparse-reward discussion (finding 18/19 + 2601.00844), and do not cite it as a cause of the encoder/dynamics-gate negatives.

findings.title: Terminal dynamics are extreme-sparse (~1 of ~301 slots changes; 27.5% of transitions change nothing), making the prediction target ~copy — but copy already explains 80% of the target norm, so the learnable residual is a thin near-orthogonal direction, not a dense signal
findings.our: Recomputed from val.jsonl state_after t vs t+1 and the frozen cache. Symbolic: state-changing transitions change mean 1.073 slots (93% change exactly 1; the 7% two-slot cases are cp/mv touching src+dst); no-op and invalid change 0 slots (verified exactly). Overall a command perturbs 3.16% of present slots (~26.5) / 0.26% of the fixed 301 universe, and 27.5% of transitions (no-op 12.4% + invalid 15.2%) perturb NOTHING. In frozen ModernBERT feature space: no-op/invalid produce EXACTLY 0.00 feature delta (perfect copy target); state-changing has copy-floor L2 median 30.88 against target norm median 155.96, i.e. copy already explains 80.5% of the target's norm, and 92% (median) of the feature delta-power sits on the single symbolically-changed slot (env/render.py:24-35 renders one line per path; models/gate2.py assembles the fixed 301-slot map).
findings.jepa: V-JEPA/PLDM/DINO-WM/V-JEPA 2-AC train on dense continuous video/robot signals where an action or camera motion perturbs a LARGE fraction of patches (agent occupies many patches; arm/camera motion moves whole regions). JEPA.md §3.2 explicitly flags the opposite regime as the danger zone: text is 'discrete and low-bandwidth, so the unpredictable-detail problem is manageable' and input-space reconstruction 'works well for text' — i.e. the abstraction advantage of latent prediction shrinks exactly when the signal is sparse/low-bandwidth, which is our regime. The recon-twin matching JEPA (finding 22) is the predicted consequence.
findings.causal: Because the target is ~80% copy and the changed signal is 1-slot-sparse, the prediction objective is dominated by copy-maintenance, and every from-scratch/pretrained feature-space predictor collapses toward the copy baseline on held-out layouts (findings 17 rounds 1-2, 21 all-4-arms, 23 genllm: state-changing 63.95 vs copy floor 63.13 — WORSE than copy). Finding 17's 'diffuse haze over the 1 slot that semantically changed' is this sparsity in continuous form. So low target density is a genuine contributor to the copy-collapse. It is NOT the whole story (see the refutation finding): a symbolic predictor over the same sparsity transfers fine.
findings.redesign: Raise per-step target density so prediction is not dominated by copy: (a) collapse multi-command 'macro-actions' into single transitions so one step moves several correlated slots; (b) generate cascade/dependency dynamics (a write that invalidates dependents, a chained mkdir+touch) so a command touches a correlated block rather than 1 line; (c) drop or heavily downweight the 27.5% no-op/invalid copy transitions in the prediction loss (keep them only for the calibration/VoE eval) so gradient isn't spent re-learning identity. Each raises signal-to-copy ratio toward the dense regime the papers train on.
findings.verify_note:
FINDING (contributing, corrected): Terminal dynamics are extreme-sparse — 27.5% of transitions change nothing (no-op 12.35% + invalid 15.18%; feature delta EXACTLY 0.00, verified), and state-changing transitions touch mean 1.073 slots (92.7% exactly 1; the 2-slot cases are cp/mv src+dst), i.e. ~3.2% of ~26 present slots / 0.26% of the 301 universe. This makes the aggregate prediction target copy-dominated: in the Frobenius/whole-map convention the copy baseline is already 80% of the way to the target (residual ||delta||/||target|| = 0.198, NOT "copy explains 80% of the norm" — that phrasing inverts residual vs explained), and 92% of the state-changing delta-power sits on the single changed slot. This is a real divergence from the dense/continuous video-robot regime the JEPA papers train on (V-JEPA temporal multi-block; PLDM/DINO-WM/V-JEPA-2-AC where an action moves a large patch fraction), and JEPA.md 3.2's "discrete, low-bandwidth" caveat applies.

BUT the causal story "the learnable residual is a thin near-orthogonal direction, not a dense signal" is MISLEADING and is the correction. The thinness is ONLY at the whole-map level (1 of ~26 present slots). ON the changed slot, delta/slot-norm = 1.0000 (delta L2 29.35 ≈ full slot norm 29.46) and the residual is near-orthogonal to the anchor (cos median -0.009) — i.e. the learnable target is a FULL-MAGNITUDE rewrite of one slot vector, a large well-defined signal, not a thin direction. The gate2 loss already up-weights changed slots 100x (gate2.py:411-413), neutralizing the whole-map dilution, so low target-norm-fraction is not what starves the objective.

CAUSAL LINK (corrected): sparsity/copy-dominance makes the AGGREGATE feature-space metric copy-dominated and the gate numerically hard, and the perfect-copy no-op/invalid targets do reward copy-maintenance — a genuine contributing condition. But the negatives themselves (finding 23 genllm state-changing 63.95 > copy floor 63.13 — WORSE than copy — captures ~0 of the residual and adds haze; finding 21 all-4-arms; finding 17 diffuse-haze) are driven by LAYOUT-ENTANGLEMENT over the frozen-feature interface: the predictor cannot localize/synthesize the one full-magnitude changed slot on HELD-OUT layouts (seen-layout monitors reach copy-ratio 0.85). Decisive counter-evidence that sparsity is NOT the load-bearing cause: a SYMBOLIC predictor over the identical sparsity (tier-2 slotpred) transfers and clears the state-changing/no-op behavior — same sparsity, different (transferable) representation, different outcome. So the wall is the frozen-feature/generalization interface, not target density.

NUMBERS (recomputed, PYTHONHASHSEED=0): symbolic state-changing mean 1.0733, {1:6447,2:510}, 92.67% exactly-1; no-op/invalid exactly 0; perfect-copy 27.53%; 3.23% of present / 0.26% of 301. Feature space: no-op/invalid delta 0.00 exact; Frobenius copy-floor median 30.884 / target 155.958 = residual 19.8% (= "80% preserved by copy"); sum-over-slots copy-floor mean 63.64 ≈ committed 63.13; 92% (median 0.914) of delta-power on the changed slot; changed-slot delta/slot-norm = 1.0000; cos(delta,anchor) = -0.009.

findings.title: The per-trajectory banner is LITERALLY the slowest feature by construction (change-fraction 0.0000 vs cwd 0.05 / tree 0.67 / noise 1.0) — the data hard-codes the 2211.10831 slow-feature trap — but the CLEAN gate-2/gate-1 pipelines that carry the actual negatives strip the banner, so it is not the cause of THIS project's held-out failures
findings.our: env/render.py:15-21 (_header) prepends vocab.BANNERS[banner_id] once; datagen/generate.py:105 draws banner_id once per trajectory and holds it fixed across all 24 steps. Recomputed per-channel temporal change-fraction over 400 val trajectories: banner 0.0000 (perfectly static), cwd 0.0505, tree body 0.6742, dynamic-noise line 1.0000. So the banner is strictly the slowest-varying channel and every signal channel changes more than it. terminal-jepa.md §3 explicitly designed it this way ('the slow-feature trap from arXiv 2211.10831 ... must vary across trajectories to let the encoder satisfy variance regularizers by copying it, while staying trivially predictable in time'). Crucially, the gate-2 pipeline renders TARGETS clean (banner=None, models/gate2.py:283, finding 15a) and the codebook/round-4/round-5 clean-input runs never expose the predictor to the banner at all.
findings.jepa: JEPA.md §5/§10 and 2211.10831: JEPA objectives implement slow-feature learning; a distractor SLOWER than the signal gets copied into the representation and satisfies the objective without learning dynamics — 'no general solution beyond data diversity.' A variance-seeking anti-collapse regularizer (VICReg/SIGReg) will preferentially grab the cheapest source of cross-sample variance that costs zero prediction error, and a per-trajectory-static-but-across-trajectory-varying banner is exactly that: free variance, zero temporal-prediction cost.
findings.causal: The construction is a valid and faithful analog of the trap (the intuition (2) is well-founded, not ill-conceived), and it BIT in the slot recipe: finding 12 measured the trained slot encoder decoding banner identity at 19x chance with banner-swap ratio 3.22 (red line 0.5), because its 25:1 variance hinge needed cross-sample variance and the banner was the cheapest source — a clean text-domain replication of 2211.10831. BUT it did NOT cause the load-bearing negatives (findings 13, 23): the CLS recipe suppressed it (banner-swap ratio 0.075, banner acc ~chance), and the frozen-feature gate-2 pipeline that produces the copy-collapse negatives trains and evaluates on clean-rendered targets with the banner absent. So the banner is a correctly-built stress test that fires only under strong variance pressure; it is not the mechanism behind the held-out-transfer wall.
findings.redesign: Keep the banner strictly as an RQ2 instrument, not in the main training target. To make it a genuinely load-bearing trap worth studying, raise its entropy toward the paper's full-frame scale (multi-line banner, more bits) AND put it in the prediction TARGET (not clean-stripped) so a variance-seeking recipe actually pays to ignore it — otherwise the clean-target design guarantees the trap can never bite the gate-2 arm. Separately, to defeat it where it does bite (slot recipe), the corpus prescription is the IDM auxiliary (2211.10831 found inverse dynamics robust to exactly this distractor) plus data diversity.
findings.verify_note: CONFIRMED with one phrasing correction. The banner IS the slowest feature by construction (recomputed change-fraction 0.0000 vs cwd 0.0500 / tree 0.6737 / noise 1.0000 over 400 val trajectories; generate.py:105 draws it once per trajectory, render.py:15-18 prepends it every step) and materializes as a real slow contamination in feature space too (banner-only spillover median dz 2.23 on present slots, ~83% a constant within-trajectory offset — larger than the genuine changed-slot temporal signal, median 1.06), so it is a faithful text-domain replication of arXiv 2211.10831 and intuition (2) is well-founded. It BIT only where the trap's mechanism exists: the slot JEPA recipe's 25:1-variance-hinge + per-index SIGReg (finding 12: banner acc 29.3%=19x, swap ratio 3.22), and was suppressed by the CLS recipe's weak variance pressure (0.075, acc 1.26%). It is NOT the cause of the load-bearing held-out negatives: (a) the gate-2 frozen-feature pipeline has NO variance regularizer (gate2.py:406-413 is pure weighted L2), renders TARGETS clean (banner absent, gate2.py:326/:283 — so copying the banner raises loss), and EVALUATES clean (dynamics.py:381 + run_round4/round5 scripts all pass --input-regime clean), so the copy-collapse negatives (round-5 state-changing copy_ratio ~0.83, AUC ~0.80) are measured with the banner absent; (b) the decisive dissociation in finding 13 — the banner-CAPTURING slot arm (28.0% held-out macro) fails no worse than the banner-SUPPRESSING CLS arm (26.0%) — shows transfer failure is orthogonal to banner capture. CORRECTION to the finding's wording: the gate-2 predictor is not entirely banner-free in training — gate2.py:324-325 feeds dirty (banner-contaminated) INPUTS 50% of draws, but always against clean targets with no variance regularizer, so the banner is a denoising burden, not a copyable slow feature. That refinement strengthens the finding. The banner is a correctly-built stress test that fires under variance pressure but is inert in the pipelines carrying THIS project's held-out-transfer wall; the wall is the frozen-feature interface / symbolic-decode bottleneck (findings 21/23), not the banner.

findings.title: Discreteness/non-smoothness of the STATE does not block dynamics learning: a symbolic predictor over the identical discrete, 1-slot-sparse dynamics transfers near-perfectly to held-out layouts — so the wall is the frozen continuous-feature interface, not discreteness per se
findings.our: tier-2 slotpred (models/tier2.py) predicts over a discrete symbolic slot representation (fixed key universe, one-hot content classes) — the SAME 1.07-slot sparsity, 27.5% copy transitions, and discontinuous symbol switching as the frozen-feature target. Its hardened 5-seed held-out battery (runs/tier2/slotpred-v2/battery-hardened.json): state-changing h1 mean 0.277+/-0.011, no-op 0.000, AUC ~0.906. The oracle (runs/dynamics-battery/oracle-oracle-hardened.json) is exactly 0 error / AUC 1.0. Meanwhile the frozen-continuous-feature predictors (round 4 all four arms finding 21; round 5 genllm finding 23: state-changing 63.95 vs copy 63.13) fail on the same held-out layouts.
findings.jepa: The slow-features/temporal-straightening/PLDM continuity priors (2211.10831, 2603.12231, 2502.14819) are properties of the LATENT SPACE and the objective, not of the underlying task. A discrete task can still be modeled by a discrete predictor; JEPA.md §8 notes 'essentially every component varies' across JEPA instantiations and the portable core is 'predict in representation space' — nothing requires the representation to be continuous. The continuity assumption is specifically a property of the frozen-continuous-encoder + smooth-planner recipe, not of world-modeling in general.
findings.causal: This REFUTES the strong form of intuition (1) ('discreteness breaks the smoothness inductive bias these methods rely on, therefore the task fails'). The task is learnable and transfers when the representation is symbolic/discrete; it fails specifically when dynamics must be learned over frozen CONTINUOUS ModernBERT features, where the copy-floor is 80% of the target norm and the learnable residual is a non-transferable near-orthogonal direction (finding 23's 'frozen-feature interface is the wall'). So discreteness is a mismatch for the smoothness-exploiting METHODS (planning), but not the cause of the representation/dynamics-transfer negatives. The honest framing: the continuity priors are inapplicable here, but their inapplicability is survivable by choosing a discrete predictor, which the project already demonstrated.
findings.redesign: Lean into the discrete representation rather than fighting to force continuity: the working pipeline is frozen-features -> linear/codebook symbol grounding -> SYMBOLIC discrete dynamics (finding 17 round 3 / tier-2). Treat the smoothness-exploiting JEPA planners as out-of-scope for this modality and report that as a scoped negative ('continuity priors are inapplicable to discrete symbolic terminal state'), while pursuing the discrete-dynamics + value-shaped-cost path for planning. If a continuous latent is required for a specific method, first make the dynamics denser/smoother (macro-actions, cascades) so consecutive velocities acquire the correlation those methods need.
findings.verify_note:
CONFIRMED (top-line) with a corrected mechanism. The dissociation is real and decisive: a symbolic discrete predictor over the identical 1.07-slot-sparse, 27.5%-copy discrete dynamics transfers near-perfectly to genuinely held-out layouts (tier2 slotpred: state-changing 0.277±0.011, no-op 0.000, AUC 0.906, 5 seeds; oracle 0.0/1.0; train/val layout overlap = 0 — verified), whereas learned predictors over frozen CONTINUOUS ModernBERT features fail on the same held-out layouts (finding 21 all four arms; finding 23 genllm 63.95 vs copy 63.13). This refutes the STRONG form of intuition-1 — discreteness/non-smoothness of the STATE does not block dynamics learning or transfer. The smoothness/slow-feature/temporal-straightening priors (2211.10831, 2603.12231, 2502.14819) are latent-space/objective properties, not task properties; JEPA.md §8's 'predict in representation space is the portable core' does not require a continuous representation. So the continuity priors are inapplicable here but their inapplicability is survivable by choosing a discrete predictor — which the project demonstrated. Discreteness is a mismatch for the smoothness-EXPLOITING methods (gradient planning), not the cause of the representation/dynamics-transfer negatives.

HOWEVER, the finding's stated CAUSAL LINK is wrong on specifics and must be rewritten:
1. NOT 'copy-floor is 80% of the target norm.' Recomputed: copy-floor 63.6 is 7.8% of target norm 819.6 (battery units); copy retains ~92% of the way to target. The 79-80% figure is finding-15a's nuisance-power/signal-power ratio, a different quantity, misimported.
2. NOT 'a non-transferable near-orthogonal residual.' On the symbolically-changed slot the residual is near-PARALLEL (cos 0.99, 8.1°), SMALL (13% of target norm), and mostly TRANSFERABLE (per-verb direction consistency 0.72-0.98 for mkdir/touch/rm/write/cp; only cd and mv diffuse).
3. The real wall: the frozen-feature target is NOT 1.07-slot sparse. A 1-symbol edit moves ~14.3 ModernBERT line-vectors (bidirectional contextual contamination via token-mean line pooling), and ~57% of the target-change L2 mass is spurious contextual drift on symbolically-unchanged lines — and that drift is layout-idiosyncratic. That layout-dependent densification of a symbolically-sparse target is what defeats cross-layout transfer, matching findings 17/21/23's 'diffuse-haze' + 'copy-ratio 0.85 seen / worse-than-copy held-out' layout-entanglement. So the honest framing is: the frozen CONTINUOUS ENCODER converts a sparse, action-determined, transferable symbolic target into a dense, layout-entangled one — not that 'continuous representation' per se is unlearnable (that is bounded to this encoder/target-design/scale: single training seed, targets from the same ModernBERT rather than a separate text encoder, <=316M predictor). This makes the finding STRONGER and more precise while removing its two incorrect numbers.

findings.title: The clean full-obs domain has ZERO unpredictable detail to abstract away — precisely the regime JEPA.md §3.2/§9 say JEPA has no edge in; the manufactured 'detail' is 6.7% of chars and not even conditionally unpredictable
findings.our: env/actions.py:50 apply() is a pure deterministic function of (state,action); env/render.py:24 render_full is a deterministic bijection on symbolic state (parse is its tested inverse). Measured on data/v1 val (bandwidth.py): clean state serialization = 322 chars mean (90-639); banner line = 32 chars (8.5%, per-trajectory CONSTANT so fully predictable in time); dynamic-noise line = 26 chars (6.7%). Total manufactured 'detail to discard' = 15.2% of chars, of which only the 6.7% noise is unpredictable-given-state. And env/render.py:10 _noise_line is a deterministic f(noise_seed, step) with noise_seed=traj index (datagen/generate.py:116) — so it is fully predictable given (traj_id, step), uncorrelated with state, ~2 tokens. It is a distractor, not irreducible stochastic detail.
findings.jepa: JEPA.md §3.2: 'tokens are discrete and low-bandwidth, so the unpredictable-detail problem is manageable... [pixel reconstruction] breaks down for continuous, high-bandwidth signals like video.' The entire raison d'etre (§1, §2 point 4, §9 finding 1) is that a target ENCODER can discard conditionally-unpredictable high-bandwidth detail (grass texture, leaf motion) that a generative model is forced to commit to. V-JEPA's +5.1 latent-over-pixel gain (§3.2) and the physics +51% gain (§3.2, 2603.13227) all come from signals with genuine irreducible detail. The papers explicitly reserve NO edge for discrete low-bandwidth text (§7 LLM-JEPA/VL-JEPA only ADD a JEPA term to next-token loss; §3.2 note: 'a JEPA produces representations, not samples').
findings.causal: A JEPA's advantage is a target encoder that throws away detail a reconstruction loss would waste capacity on. Here the clean target has no such detail: given (state, action) the next clean observation is 100% determined and bijective with the state a probe wants to read. So the target encoder's abstraction step has nothing to buy — which is exactly why the reconstruction twin sits at parity (status finding 22) and why finding 13 shows trained encoders decode held-out state WORSE than random features (26.0/28.0 vs 28.2/28.4 macro): abstraction in a zero-irreducible-detail regime can only DESTROY the linearly-available symbols, never add value. This subsumes the parity finding (22) and the trained-below-untrained finding (13) under one cause: we are testing JEPA where its core value proposition is definitionally absent.
findings.redesign: To put JEPA in-regime you must inject GENUINELY irreducible, high-bandwidth, conditionally-unpredictable detail that CORRELATES with state (so abstracting it helps) — not a state-independent noise line. E.g. render file CONTENTS as long, stochastic, state-conditioned text (a content class = a distribution over many-token bodies, sampled fresh each render) so the semantic class is predictable but the surface form is not; then reconstruction must model unpredictable bytes while JEPA can abstract to the class. Absent such detail, the honest move is to drop JEPA-vs-reconstruction as the question and adopt the frozen-encoder+symbolic-decoder pipeline (finding 17) as the product, since it already solves representation.
findings.verify_note: The divergence is REAL and the numbers reproduce (clean~331 chars, banner 8.97%, noise 7.14%, manufactured 16.1%; ~0.78 of 301 slots change per transition, 27.5% change zero; recon-twin 0.542 vs frozen 0.444 vs trained-JEPA 0.260 vs untrained 0.282 — all verified against artifacts; determinism/bijectivity claims verified at env/actions.py:50, env/render.py:10-35, env/parse.py:15). It is LOAD-BEARING for exactly one thing: the reconstruction-twin PARITY (finding 22) and the fact that clean full-obs cannot give JEPA an edge — this matches JEPA.md §3.2/§9 (low-bandwidth text, no irreducible detail to discard) AND the project's own pre-registration (terminal-jepa.md:44, :49 declare clean-obs a parsing smoke test where twin parity is expected). But its causal-subsumption claim (that this one cause also explains the held-out trained-below-untrained transfer failures, findings 13/21/23) is an OVER-REACH: those are driven by a distinct axis — held-out layout-entanglement (findings 3/5/10) plus a small-predictor generative-synthesis burden over a FROZEN feature interface (finding 17 rounds 1-2, findings 21/23, which contain no JEPA abstraction step at all, so 'the abstraction step has nothing to buy' cannot be their cause; finding 23's own verdict is 'the frozen-feature interface is the wall, not the predictor model'). Two refinements to the finding's wording: (1) the manufactured detail is NOT a trivial ~2-token isolated append in the representation the model consumes — ModernBERT contextualization spreads the banner+noise header to whole-map L2 16.4 (~57% of a real state change) across ~25 path-line slots (corroborating finding 15a's 'nuisance = 79% of signal power'), so there IS a real input-side distractor to abstract away — but the project renders CLEAN targets, so the TARGET still carries zero irreducible detail and the finding's target-side claim survives; (2) the JEPA-relevant test (JEPA vs reconstruction in the nuisance-INPUT regime, terminal-jepa.md:88) was never run, so 'JEPA definitionally has no edge here' is established for the clean-target regime only and remains a hypothesis, not a verdict, for the nuisance regime the papers actually target. Corrected verdict: a real, contributing divergence that decisively explains the parity result but is not the single/subsuming cause of the transfer negatives.
