findings.title: The frozen-feature interface INVERTS the sparsity problem instead of fixing it: a 1-of-301 symbolic edit becomes a dense 27-of-27-line contextual-drift target that is ~1/27 semantic and ~26/27 nuisance — a needle the small predictor cannot synthesize, so it collapses to copy
findings.our: Recomputed on runs/gate2/cache-val.pt (clean ModernBERT features, 301x768/state) + data/v1 (context_spread.py, feature_change.py): a state-changing transition changes SYMBOLICALLY mean 1.08/median 1 slot (max 2), but in ModernBERT feature space moves mean 27.31/median 27 of the ~26.5 present line-vectors (103% of present lines) via bidirectional contextualization. Feature-space copy floor for state-changing = 63.64 (matches status finding 21's 63.13). The predictor (models/gate2.py Gate2Net/Gate2GenLLMNet) must regress this dense target; over 301x768 dims 90.9% of the target mass is trivially copy (absent slots=0), and of the present lines only ~1/27 carries the semantic change.
findings.jepa: In V-JEPA/PLDM/DINO-WM/V-JEPA 2-AC (JEPA.md §4-§5) an action or camera motion changes ALL or a large contiguous PATCH of the target — the action-observation coupling is dense and the change IS the signal. V-JEPA's multi-block masking (§9 finding 2: 72.9 vs 51.5 random-tube on K400) exists precisely because the masked target is a large predictable region, not a needle. Their copy-input baseline scores near-zero. VL-JEPA (§7) uses a ~490M predictor to synthesize 768-d text-semantic targets — the generative burden our 0.64M-316M nets cannot carry (status finding 17 round-2 diagnosis).
findings.causal: This is the direct mechanism behind the dynamics-gate failures. Because the true-next feature target and an alt-action feature target both differ from f(s_t) by ~1 semantic slot buried in ~26 slots of SHARED contextual drift, the copy prediction f(s_t) is roughly equidistant from both (measured copy VoE 0.492, exact chance; voe_mechanism.py). To beat copy the predictor must SYNTHESIZE the correct 1-of-27 contextual re-embedding, a generative act. The faithful pretrained SmolLM2-360M predictor (finding 23) instead lands at state-changing h1=63.95 ~ copy 63.13, AUC 0.803<0.9, VoE 0.525~chance — it copies plus residual haze. So the 'frozen-feature interface is the wall' verdict is caused by contextualization converting a sparse discrete signal into a dense nuisance-dominated target, not by the predictor's family/scale.
findings.redesign: Do not regress raw 768-d contextual line-vectors. Either (a) predict in the SYMBOL/CODE space the linear decoder already recovers 100% (finding 17's codebook pipeline is the proof this works) — i.e. make the JEPA target the grounded discrete state, not the contextual embedding; or (b) if a learned latent is wanted, use a per-path/slot target that does NOT drift under unrelated edits — e.g. encode each line INDEPENDENTLY (no cross-line attention) or with an explicitly order/context-invariant per-path encoder (A3's path-keyed equivariance), so a 1-slot edit produces a 1-slot target change and the signal is no longer a needle.
findings.verify_note: CORRECTED FINDING (bandwidth-abstraction-regime): The frozen-feature interface does turn a 1-of-301 symbolic edit into a target where all ~27 present line-vectors drift under bidirectional contextualization — this is real and reproduces (copy floor 63.64; VoE geometry ||f_t-f_next||=62.30, ||f_next-f_alt||=96.58, copy VoE~0.42-0.49 chance; finding-23 genllm 63.95/1.65/2.63/AUC0.803 all confirmed). BUT the drift is NOT nuisance that drowns the semantic needle: the edited slot moves ~20x more than drift lines (26.5 vs 1.3) and carries 87% of the squared-delta energy (median 92%); only under the battery's sum-of-per-slot-L2 distance does the aggregated 26-line drift reach 55% of the copy floor. Decisive counter-evidence: (1) an EDIT-ONLY oracle predictor that regresses ONLY the edited slot and COPIES the 26 drift lines cuts rollout 62.3->34.0 and scores VoE 0.97 — so beating copy does NOT require synthesizing a dense 27-line target; getting the one edited slot right suffices, and the shared drift cancels between true-next and alt. (2) The actual trained predictor on HELD-OUT layouts moves the edited slot by ~0.001 (essentially copy) and adds negligible drift haze — it is not drowning the needle in nuisance, it is failing to LOCALIZE/REGRESS the one edit. (3) On SEEN layouts the same predictor reaches copy_ratio 0.85 on state-changing — the generative burden is surmountable in-distribution. So the true mechanism is the project's LAYOUT-TRANSFER / identifiability wall (findings 3/10/11/13) reappearing at the dynamics level, not a nuisance-bandwidth ceiling. The nuisance-domination story is a real but SECONDARY artifact of the sum-of-L2 metric + the loss's inability to isolate 1 changed slot from 300 (edit-weight fails at both 0 and 100). Separately, the per-trajectory banner + dynamic-noise DISTRACTOR (intuition 2) is a legitimate-but-weak 2211.10831 analog (~3% of tokens) and is largely MOOT for these gate failures, because gate-2 renders targets CLEAN (models/gate2.py:283) and the linear codebook strips input nuisance — so the distractor cannot be causing the dynamics-gate negatives under review. Reframe the finding from "frozen-feature interface inverts sparsity into an unsynthesizable dense nuisance target" to "frozen contextualized features make the single semantic edit predictable IN-DISTRIBUTION but not IDENTIFIABLE across held-out layouts; the sum-of-L2 metric additionally penalizes any residual drift haze, so a predictor that fails to transfer collapses to copy-plus-haze." The corrective levers this implies (A2 grounded temporally-masked prediction, A3 state-equivalence invariance, per-slot/edit-localized loss, or a predicate-masked distance) target identifiability/transfer, not target bandwidth.

findings.title: The bandwidth-regime story does NOT subsume the central negative: the wall is held-out-LAYOUT compositional transfer, and the reconstruction twin hits it just as hard (content-given-existence 11.7%, BELOW the 17.5% chance floor) — a binding problem orthogonal to bandwidth
findings.our: runs/recon-v1-s0-4k/probe-v2.json (the finding-22 twin): held-out pooled linear macro 0.542 decomposes as cwd 0.932, existence-balacc 0.578 (~chance 0.5), content-given-existence 0.117 — BELOW the 17.5% majority floor. So on genuine per-file state the reconstruction twin is at or below chance on held-out layouts; its whole macro edge is the single cwd scalar (a global token). Trained JEPA held-out macro 0.260 (sigreg) / 0.280 (slot) sit at or below untrained 0.282/0.284 (runs/untrained-{cls,slot}-v1-v2.json). Frozen ModernBERT under path-keyed readout already gives content 100% held-out (finding 14) — but that is the parser/line-keying, not learned dynamics.
findings.jepa: The bandwidth argument (JEPA.md §3.2) predicts JEPA has no EDGE over reconstruction in low-bandwidth text — a claim about the JEPA-vs-recon DELTA. It does NOT predict that reconstruction itself fails to transfer. PLDM (§5) generalizes to unseen maze layouts from 5 layouts by DENSE pixel dynamics; the transfer there is spatial-metric, and no JEPA-corpus method faces our compositional symbol-recombination held-out-layout split (terminal-jepa.md §3 notes this is novel: 'PLDM's pixel-maze layouts never face this distinction; symbolic domains do'). The slow-feature paper (§5, 2211.10831) and the identifiability gap are separate failure axes.
findings.causal: If bandwidth-regime were THE meta-explanation, fixing bandwidth would fix everything. But the twin — which lives in exactly the regime where reconstruction is supposed to be fine — still cannot decode held-out file state (11.7% < 17.5% floor). That means a SECOND, independent cause is present: neither prediction+anti-collapse NOR reconstruction forces a path-consistent CANONICAL code that composes to new layouts (status finding 10-13 identifiability analysis). Bandwidth explains the JEPA-vs-recon PARITY and the trained-below-untrained DAMAGE; it does not explain the shared transfer FAILURE. Argument FOR the meta-hypothesis: bandwidth does subsume the two negatives about JEPA's relative value (findings 13, 22-parity) and the dynamics-gate collapse (finding 23, via finding-2 mechanism). Argument AGAINST: the product-critical negative (cross-layout absolute-state transfer, the stated product requirement) is a compositional-binding failure the recon twin shares, so it survives any bandwidth fix.
findings.redesign: Attack binding directly, independent of bandwidth: (1) A3 state-equivalence invariance (line-order permutation, banner/noise) to manufacture canonical path-keyed coordinates; (2) A2 grounded temporally-masked prediction where obs_{t-1}+action fully determines the masked region so carrying absolute state forward is the only way to win; (3) an inverse-dynamics / contrastive term keyed on PATH so 'which slot changed' composes into 'what value the slot holds'. Test the meta-hypothesis crisply: run the recon twin under a HIGH-irreducible-detail render (from finding 1's seed) — if the twin degrades while a JEPA arm holds, bandwidth is load-bearing for the delta; if BOTH still fail held-out transfer, binding is the dominant cause and bandwidth is secondary.
findings.verify_note:
The bandwidth-regime story is REAL and load-bearing for two of the three negatives, but it does NOT subsume the product-critical one — confirming the finding, with a sharpening. Independently re-derived numbers all reproduce:

1. BANDWIDTH IS LOAD-BEARING FOR: (a) the dynamics-gate collapse — recomputed from val.jsonl, state-changing transitions move a MEDIAN 1 of 301 symbolic slots (mean 1.11, 0.37%); no-ops/invalids move 0. The target is ~copy, and the genllm-pretrained predictor lands at the copy floor (63.95 vs recomputed 63.64), i.e. collapse-to-copy (finding 23). (b) the JEPA-vs-recon parity + trained-below-untrained damage: JEPA held-out macro 26-28% ≤ untrained 28.2%/28.4%; recon content 11.7% is 3.2 pts below untrained-CLS (15.0%). These match JEPA.md §3.2's prediction (no JEPA edge in low-bw text) exactly.

2. BANDWIDTH IS A RED HERRING FOR the product wall (cross-layout absolute-state transfer). Decisive control I ran: POOLED single-vector content-given-existence is at/below the 17.5% floor for EVERY objective — recon 11.7%, frozen-pretrained-768d 16.1%, untrained-CLS 15.0%, trained-JEPA ~floor — while PATH-KEYED per-line content is 100% for BOTH pretrained AND random-init ModernBERT. Same encoder, same information, only pooling differs: the content EXISTS in token states (100% per-line; recon decoder reaches 0.50 CE), and pooling destroys the path↔content BINDING, not the information. This is a binding/identifiability failure (findings 10-13), objective- and bandwidth-independent, that survives any bandwidth fix. Supported by the delta dissociation (arg1/which-path-touched 21.5% = 8.8x chance transfers, absolute state at floor).

REFINEMENT the finding understates: (i) the feature-space target is NOT literally sparse — a 1-symbol change hazes across ~14.6 ModernBERT slots (42% signal / 58% haze), so the sparsity mechanism bites via the CONTEXTUAL representation, not raw-data copy; and (ii) the recon twin shares the 256-d CLS bottleneck with JEPA, so their content parity alone can't separate "bottleneck" from "binding" — it is the POOLED-FROZEN-768d-at-floor-vs-its-own-per-line-100% contrast that proves the wall is binding, not capacity/bandwidth. Verdict stands: two negatives are bandwidth, the product-critical one is binding.

findings.title: The banner+dynamic-noise distractor is a valid but WEAK and mis-typed analog to the JEPA papers' distractors — it manufactures 'detail to discard' that is too low-bandwidth to matter and, being state-independent, cannot reward abstraction the way a real slow feature does
findings.our: env/render.py:10-21: banner = 1 line from a 64-entry vocab (vocab.py: '### {word} terminal build {NNN} ###', ~6 bits/32 chars), held fixed per trajectory (datagen/generate.py:116 banner_id per traj); dynamic noise = 1 line '[ts N] [pid N]' resampled per step, a deterministic f(traj_id, step) uncorrelated with state. Together 15.2% of chars, 6.7% unpredictable-given-state (bandwidth.py). Status finding 2/12: the CLS recipe SUPPRESSES the banner (swap ratio 0.075-0.118) while the slot recipe CAPTURES it (19x chance, ratio 3.22) — so the trap is recipe-dependent and the audit (status doc, 'RQ2 caveat') already concedes it is 'a far weaker trap than 2211.10831's full-frame distractor (one line, ~3% of tokens, 6 bits vs image-scale noise).' Frozen features leak the banner (pooled banner-swap ratio 1.17, banner decodable 45%; runs/frozen-modernbert-v1/probe.json).
findings.jepa: 2211.10831 (JEPA.md §5, §9-finding-1, §10) shows the slow-feature trap: a distractor SLOWER than the signal (a fixed noise BACKGROUND spanning the whole frame) gets copied into the representation and satisfies the objective without learning dynamics. The load-bearing properties are (a) it is a LARGE fraction of the high-bandwidth input and (b) it is slower/more-static than the task signal so variance regularizers latch onto it. V-JEPA's leaf-motion / grass-texture distractors are genuine irreducible detail, not a bolted-on string.
findings.causal: The banner is a legitimate DIRECTION of the 2211.10831 trap (the untrained encoder does latch it 5-7x chance, and the slot recipe captures it 19x — status finding 12 confirms the paper's prediction), so the concept is not ill-conceived. But two mismatches make it non-load-bearing for our MAIN negatives: (1) at 6 bits / one line it is too small to dominate a 322-char state serialization, so suppressing or capturing it barely moves probe transfer (findings 2, 13 hold with banner at chance); (2) it is state-INDEPENDENT, so 'abstract the banner away' is a free win that does not exercise JEPA's actual value (abstracting UNPREDICTABLE-BUT-STATE-CORRELATED detail). Thus the distractor is a cosmetic-to-contributing confound, not the cause of the held-out-transfer or dynamics-gate failures — it neither creates nor fixes them.
findings.redesign: If a distractor is wanted that actually tests JEPA's value proposition, make it (a) HIGH-bandwidth (many tokens, e.g. a multi-line log preamble that dwarfs the state) and (b) either genuinely stochastic per-render OR state-CORRELATED-but-surface-unpredictable (so throwing it away requires learning the semantic invariant). The per-trajectory banner is fine as a slow-feature probe but should be relabeled a diagnostic, not a stand-in for the papers' distractors; the dynamic-noise line, being a deterministic distractor uncorrelated with state, should not be counted as 'unpredictable detail' at all.
findings.verify_note:
FINDING (contributing, verdict upheld with corrections): The banner+dynamic-noise distractor is a VALID but WEAK and MIS-TYPED analog of the JEPA papers' distractors — and it is a confound, not a cause, of the project's negatives.

VALID DIRECTION (confirmed): The banner is a legitimate instance of 2211.10831's slow-feature trap (static-within-trajectory = slow, varying-across-trajectory = free variance for the anti-collapse regularizer to copy). The slot recipe latches it at 19× chance (banner_acc 0.293, swap ratio 3.22 vs 0.5 red line; finding 12) — a genuine text-domain replication of the paper's prediction. The concept is not ill-conceived.

TWO LOAD-BEARING MIS-TYPINGS (confirmed in code): (1) At 6 bits / one line over a ~330-char state serialization it is far too small to dominate the SYMBOLIC probe target — recomputed banner 8.47% + noise 6.80% of render chars (render.py:10-35, n=9,600), banner entropy 6.0 bits (vocab.py:29-32). (2) It is STATE-INDEPENDENT (banner = f(banner_id) fixed per traj, generate.py:105; noise = f(noise_seed,step), render.py:10-12), so 'abstract the banner away' is a free win that never exercises JEPA's real value — abstracting unpredictable-but-state-CORRELATED detail (V-JEPA's leaf-motion under camera pan). It cannot reward abstraction the way an irreducible slow feature does.

NOT CAUSAL FOR THE NEGATIVES (decisive, re-derived): banner status is orthogonal to the transfer/dynamics failures. Banner-SUPPRESSING arms (CLS-trained macro 0.260 < floor 0.282; recon twin content 0.117 < 0.175 floor) fail transfer identically to the banner-CAPTURING slot arm (0.280 = floor); the arm that leaks the banner MOST (frozen path-keyed) transfers PERFECTLY (100%). Gate-2 clean vs dirty is numerically indistinguishable (state-chg h1 0.297 vs 0.300; invalid clause fails at 0.181 either way) because the linear decode strips nuisance. Suppressing or capturing the distractor neither creates nor fixes the held-out-transfer or dynamics-gate failures.

REFINEMENT (challenges the finding's 'too low-bandwidth' framing without overturning it): in FROZEN-FEATURE space (cache-val.pt, 301×768) the nuisance is NOT negligible — per-slot contamination dz 2.72 vs changed-line 3.59 (SNR 1.3×), aggregate nuisance ≈5.8× signal power (echoes finding 15a). The banner fails to matter not because it is small in feature space but because finding-17's linear decode removes it. This makes the distractor a real feature-space confound that is nonetheless outcome-orthogonal — still contributing, still not load-bearing.

CITATION CORRECTIONS (evidence only, verdict unchanged): (a) bandwidth.py cited in OUR-SETUP/EVIDENCE does NOT exist — the numbers are recomputable from render.py + val.jsonl, cite those. (b) '5-7× chance' untrained latching is the superseded v1 number; under current v2 the untrained encoder latches at ~14× chance (banner_acc 0.224/0.228). (c) The '6.7% unpredictable-given-state' figure counts only the per-step noise; because the banner is also state-independent across trajectories, ~15% of chars are unpredictable-given-state.

BOTTOM LINE: cosmetic-to-contributing confound, not the cause. The real load-bearing divergences live in other dimensions — the target≈copy sparsity (mean 0.778 symbolic edits/transition = 0.26% of 301 slots, 27.5% zero-edit, 92.7% single-slot) and the frozen-feature interface wall (finding 21/23) — which this distractor finding correctly does not claim.

findings.title: The banner is a VALID slow-feature trap on the temporal axis but a weak one on coverage; on the frozen-feature interface it is NOT weak in magnitude (44% of dynamics energy), refuting 'distractor too weak' there
findings.our: env/render.py:10-21 injects a per-trajectory-static banner (env/vocab.py:29-32: '### <word> terminal build NNN ###', 64 strings, one _BANNER_WORDS of 8 cycled) + a per-step dynamic '[ts N] [pid N]' noise line. datagen/generate.py:105 samples banner_id ONCE per trajectory (held fixed), noise_seed=i resamples every step. Measured on data/v1 val: banner = 8 tokens = 3.26% of a 270-token obs (median), noise = 12 tokens = 4.53%, combined header = 7.8%; banner entropy = 6 bits (64 distinct). Banner is static across all 24 obs of a trajectory (measured: every val traj is exactly 24 steps).
findings.jepa: 2211.10831 ('JEPAs Focus on Slow Features', JEPA.md §5/§10) shows the objective is satisfied by copying a distractor into the representation ONLY when the distractor is SLOWER than the signal AND covers enough of the input to dominate the prediction target. Their canonical trap is a FULL-FRAME background noise field slower than the moving signal — 100% coverage, maximal slowness.
findings.causal: On the SLOWNESS axis our banner is a faithful analog: static across ~24 consecutive frames = maximally slow, exactly the 2211.10831 condition, and status finding 12 confirms the trap fires when the recipe demands variance (slot arm: banner decoded at 29.3% = 19× chance, banner-swap ratio 3.22). On the COVERAGE axis it is far weaker than a full-frame distractor (3% of tokens vs 100% of pixels) — the status doc's own RQ2 caveat (terminal-jepa-status.md:132). BUT 'weak coverage in token space' does not mean 'weak in the space the model optimizes': on the frozen ModernBERT path-keyed features (the gate2 interface, the actual wall), the static banner alone displaces the 301x768 slot tensor by mean L2 12.58/state — 44% of the 28.8 mean state-changing dynamics energy — because contextualization smears an out-of-band header line onto every in-band tree/cwd slot vector. So the distractor is a legitimate, non-trivial nuisance on the interface that matters; it is not the toy the token-fraction number suggests.
findings.redesign: The 3%-coverage weakness is real but fixable without changing the concept: raise banner coverage/entropy (multi-line banner: hostname + build id + MOTD + shell-init lines, interleaved BEFORE and AFTER the tree so it cannot be windowed out) so static-nuisance coverage approaches 2211.10831's full-frame regime. The status doc already queues a 'higher-entropy multi-line banner knob' — do that, and make it span the observation rather than sit in a strippable header block.
findings.verify_note:
FINDING (red-herring for the negatives; the banner is a valid slow-feature trap in the ABSTRACT but is neutralized by design in every scored gate-2 arm). The banner is a faithful slow-feature analog on the TEMPORAL/slowness axis only: static across all 24 frames of every val trajectory (confirmed 400/400), 64 banners, 6-bit entropy — exactly 2211.10831's 'slower than the signal' condition. On COVERAGE it is far weaker than a full-frame distractor (banner 9 tok/3.33%, noise 12 tok/4.62%, header 8.7% of a ~264-tok obs; confirmed). On the frozen ModernBERT interface the RAW contamination is genuinely non-trivial in magnitude — banner-only whole-state L2 = 12.87 (44.7% of the 28.80 state-changing dynamics energy; both numbers reproduced exactly), because contextualization smears the header line onto every in-band slot vector. So 'distractor too weak to matter in magnitude' is refuted on RAW features.

BUT the finding's headline 'banner-swap ratio 2.26 > red-line' is a metric artifact and does not survive scrutiny: it averages dense nuisance over ~26 present slots (numerator) against a genuine ~1-slot edit diluted over the same ~26 slots (denominator 1.354). On the natural JEPA latent metric (whole-state L2) the ratio is 0.58 combined / 0.45 banner-only — banner alone is BELOW the 0.5 red line; on the exact gate2 distance (sum per-slot L2) it is 1.26. The ratio swings 0.45→2.26 by aggregation choice alone, so 2.26 is not a robust 'trap dominates' verdict.

DECISIVELY, the distractor is NOT causally load-bearing for the negatives this review concerns: every scored gate-2 negative (findings 21/23, rounds 4-5, AUC 0.803, no-op/invalid haze, held-out copy-collapse) ran --input-regime clean (artifacts self-report adapter: gate2-clean), and ALL gate-2 targets render clean (finding 15a; models/gate2.py:283, 588-595). The banner and noise are stripped from both inputs and targets in exactly those runs — a distractor absent from the evaluation cannot produce its failures. The slow-feature mechanism further requires the distractor to be copyable into the TARGET, which the clean-target design forbids; finding 17 already showed the decode path strips it (0.300 vs 0.297). The raw-contamination 44% is therefore load-bearing only for the finding-15a decision to render targets clean (a decision it correctly justifies) — it is upstream of and neutralized by that choice.

The one place the trap genuinely fires — finding 12's from-scratch slot ENCODER (banner 29.3% = 19x chance, swap 3.22) — is a Track-B end-to-end-trained encoder that can copy the banner to satisfy variance regularizers; it is a different arm from the frozen-feature Track-A negatives and does not implicate the banner in those. Corrected verdict: the distractor divergence is real and honestly measured but is a red herring for the standing negatives; the load-bearing wall is the frozen-feature interface + layout entanglement (findings 22-23), a conclusion reached with nuisance provably absent.

findings.title: The gate2/frozen-feature arm renders TARGETS clean, so the banner is NEVER in the prediction target — this BREAKS the 2211.10831 mechanism and makes the distractor irrelevant to the negatives on the interface that is the wall
findings.our: models/gate2.py:283,326-327: precompute renders BOTH a 'dirty' (banner+noise) and a 'clean' (banner=None,noise=None) view per state; batch_from (gate2.py:319-331) sets tgts and the copy anchor to tr['clean'][t+1]/tr['clean'][t] ALWAYS, and only the INPUT is sampled 50/50 clean/dirty (gate2.py:324). probes/frozen_probe.py:81-101 keys banner/noise lines as kind='banner'/'noise' index -1, so those lines are dropped from every path-keyed slot vector (they leak only via attention).
findings.jepa: 2211.10831's failure REQUIRES the distractor to be in the PREDICTION TARGET: the loss is satisfied because the slow background is present in the target encoder's output and the predictor copies it. If the target is scrubbed of the distractor, there is nothing to copy — the slow-feature trap cannot operate. The papers' distractors are always in-band (part of the pixels the target encoder sees).
findings.causal: This is a load-bearing DISANALOGY that REFUTES hypothesis (2) as an explanation for the gate2/frozen negatives (findings 21–23): the arm that produced 'no learned predictor over frozen features clears the dynamics gate' has clean targets, so the banner literally cannot be the cause of its collapse-toward-copy. The status doc's own finding 23 reaches the same place ('the frozen-feature INTERFACE is the wall, not the predictor') — and it is not the distractor either, because the distractor is out of the target loss entirely. Where the trap DOES bite is the from-scratch recipe (models/data.py:100-106 renders one banner-contaminated obs used as BOTH context and target), which is the correct 2211.10831 analog and is exactly where finding 12 sees 19×-chance banner decoding. So the distractor concept is valid and mechanistically wired correctly — but only in the from-scratch arm, and that arm's negatives are a DIFFERENT failure (layout entanglement, finding 13) that persists with the distractor OFF.
findings.redesign: Decide what the distractor is FOR and wire it into the target for that arm. If RQ2 (slow-feature robustness) is the goal, the frozen-feature arm must render DIRTY targets too (or the banner must be a slot-bearing line the parser keys), otherwise the arm is not testing RQ2 at all. If the goal is a working agent, the clean-target choice is fine but then stop attributing frozen-arm negatives to the distractor.
findings.verify_note:
CONFIRMED as load-bearing and correct, with one refinement. The gate2/frozen-feature arm renders TARGETS clean (gate2.py:326 tgts=tr['clean'][t+1], :327 anchor=tr['clean'][t]; precompute via render_full(st,None,None)), and path-keyed slots exclude banner/noise lines entirely (frozen_probe.py:91,93 -> index -1; assemble_dense builds slots only from cwd/file/dir; pooled unused in the gate2 path). Recomputed from cache-val.pt: clean-vs-clean dynamics L2 = 0.0000 exactly for both valid-no-op (n=604) and invalid (n=716), anchor==target bit-identical on 1320/1320; state-changing 63.64 ≈ finding-23 copy floor 63.13. So on the exact clauses that fail the gate (no-op/invalid haze, AUC 0.803), copy is the exact minimizer and the banner is not in the target to copy — 2211.10831's slow-feature trap cannot operate. The valid analog is the from-scratch arm (data.py:105 renders one banner-contaminated obs reused as context AND target), where the trap does bite (finding 12: banner 29.3%=19x chance, swap ratio 3.22 as free SIGReg variance). And that arm's negative is a DIFFERENT failure — layout entanglement — that persists with the distractor off: CLS suppresses the banner (finding 2 ratio 0.118, banner=chance) yet still fails held-out transfer and is even 'untrained-identical' (findings 3,7,13). This REFUTES hypothesis (2) as an explanation for the gate2/frozen negatives.

ONE REFINEMENT to the finding's wording: the banner is NOT entirely absent from the frozen arm — it is present in the 50%-dirty INPUT and does perturb path-slot vectors via ModernBERT attention. Measured input-side leak = 3.06/present-slot vs 3.76/changed-slot genuine signal (~0.81 ratio, n=131k). This is a real but categorically different issue (input-noise/A3 invariance burden), NOT the target-copy slow-feature trap, and it does not rescue hypothesis (2): a clean-only regime would still leave the banner-free copy floor failing the gate, and finding 17 showed clean==dirty numerically indistinguishable once decoded. So state the claim as: 'the banner is irrelevant to the gate2 negatives via the 2211.10831 target-copy mechanism (clean targets break it); a residual input-side banner-leak of ~0.8x signal exists on dirty draws but is an invariance nuisance, not the cause of the collapse-toward-copy.' Files: models/gate2.py:283,319-331; models/data.py:100-106,166; probes/frozen_probe.py:81-101,123-137; evidence caches runs/gate2/cache-val.pt, data/v1/val.jsonl.

findings.title: The deeper distractor problem is that the SIGNAL is too clean, not that the nuisance is too weak: a sorted, class-token, deterministic tree gives the encoder nothing to abstract away, so JEPA's core value proposition is never exercised
findings.our: env/render.py:30-34 renders the tree as sorted() canonical paths with content shown as '[c{k}]' class tokens (not real bytes); env/actions.py:50 is a pure deterministic transition (invalid actions never change state, ttype derived from exact delta). Outside the injected banner+noise header there is ZERO intra-observation nuisance: no ANSI, no timestamps in the tree, no PIDs, no hostnames, no nondeterministic directory ordering, no verbose logs, no progress bars, no line-count jitter. The observation IS a lossless machine-readable serialization of the probe targets — terminal-jepa.md:44 concedes this ('the reconstruction twin sits near the probing ceiling by construction').
findings.jepa: JEPA.md §3.2 & §9.1: latent prediction beats input reconstruction precisely when the input is high-bandwidth with lots of unpredictable/irrelevant detail to abstract away (video background motion, lighting, texture). JEPA.md §3.2 flags the LLM caveat: text is 'discrete and low-bandwidth, so the unpredictable-detail problem is manageable' — i.e., the regime where reconstruction is FINE and JEPA has no edge. V-JEPA's advantage (+5.1 K400, JEPA.md:64) exists because pixels are noisy; on a noiseless serialization there is no noise to gain from discarding.
findings.causal: This directly explains finding 22 (a reconstruction twin matched/beat JEPA here) and the copy-baseline collapse: with a canonical, deterministic, class-token tree, the observation contains only signal, so a parser/reconstructor is at ceiling and abstraction buys nothing — JEPA's entire raison d'être is null. Our distractor is a 3% wart bolted onto an otherwise noiseless signal; even if we strengthen it, the 97% clean canonical tree still lets a copy/parse baseline win. REAL terminal nuisance is the opposite: nondeterministic ordering, ANSI codes, timestamps/PIDs/hostnames threaded THROUGH the state-relevant output, progress bars, verbose logs where the state-relevant bit is a needle — dense, in-band, high-entropy, and NOT strippable by a fixed line-key parser. That is a genuinely JEPA-appropriate signal (lots to abstract away) and would make the 'path-keyed readout = parser' shortcut (finding 22) impossible. So the distractor is not ill-conceived in KIND — it is ill-conceived in DOSE and PLACEMENT: a low-coverage out-of-band header on a signal that is otherwise too clean to need abstracting.
findings.redesign: Move nuisance IN-BAND and make the signal dirty: (a) render content as short pseudo-random byte strings whose content-CLASS is a needle, not a literal '[c4]' token; (b) randomize tree line ordering per render (real ls is not globally sorted) so line-keying cannot be the parser; (c) interleave timestamps/PIDs/hostnames INTO tree/command output lines rather than a separate header; (d) add verbose-log distractor lines between state lines. Then the reconstruction twin can no longer parse to ceiling (finding 22 collapses) and abstraction has something to earn — this is the 'too clean' fix, and it subsumes the banner-strengthening fix.
findings.verify_note:
The distractor-design finding is CONTRIBUTING, not load-bearing, and two of its evidence claims are wrong at the feature-space interface where JEPA learns.

WHAT SURVIVES (real): The observation IS a lossless, sorted (render.py:34), class-token (render.py:32), deterministic (actions.py:63) serialization of the probe targets. On the clean full-obs PROBING smoke test this makes a parser/reconstructor sit at ceiling, so JEPA's abstraction edge (JEPA.md §3.2/§9.1; the low-bandwidth-text caveat) is genuinely null there — exactly as terminal-jepa.md:44 pre-concedes. This plausibly explains finding 22's reconstruction-twin PARITY on that benchmark. Reproduced: 92.7% of state-changing transitions move 1 symbolic slot (mean 1.073), 27.5% of all transitions are byte-perfect copies, no-op/invalid have exactly 0.000 feature delta.

WHAT IS WRONG (refuted):
1. 'Nuisance lives 100% in the ~8% header; 92%+ of the observation is noise-free by construction.' FALSE in feature space. ModernBERT contextualization spreads the header into ~26 of ~27 state-bearing line vectors (measured mean per-slot dz≈3.13 dirty-vs-clean), and the project's own finding-15a artifact records aggregate nuisance power = 79% of signal power. The nuisance is dense and IN-BAND at the interface, not an out-of-band wart. (Header is ~10% of subword tokens, not 7.8%.)
2. 'Signal is 1-of-301 sparse.' TRUE symbolically, FALSE in features: one edit moves ~27 slots, top slot holds only ~84% of delta power (~16% contextual spill). This diffuse spill IS the 'haze' that findings 17/21/23 fail on.

WHAT IS THE RED HERRING (refuted as cause of the target negatives): The finding claims the clean signal causes the copy-baseline collapse and the dynamics-gate failures. But findings 21 and 23 — the failures being explained — were run with --input-regime clean (banner=None, noise=None; gate2.py:591-596 + round5 run script), so ZERO distractor was present in input or target. The gate fails anyway (no-op 1.65, invalid 2.63, state-chg 63.95 > copy 63.13, AUC 0.80). A distractor absent from the failing run cannot cause that failure.

THE ACTUAL LOAD-BEARING DRIVERS of the copy-collapse: (a) layout entanglement — seen-layout train monitors hit copy-ratio 0.85 while held-out collapses to copy (finding 21); (b) the generative burden of synthesizing 768-d contextual vectors that shift across ~27 in-band slots per edit (finding 17's diffuse-haze diagnosis, reproduced at 20x params in finding 23). Both are orthogonal to signal cleanliness.

PRACTICAL CONSEQUENCE: The finding's kind-vs-dose reframe is defensible for the reconstruction-parity result, and its proposed remedy (dense, in-band, non-strippable nuisance) is a legitimate way to make the domain JEPA-appropriate and to kill the path-keyed-parser shortcut. But do not expect it to fix the primary negatives: those persist on perfectly clean inputs and are about generalizing learned dynamics over frozen contextual features across layouts. Fix the transfer/generative-burden problem first; strengthening the distractor addresses a different, milder negative (probing parity), not the copy-baseline dynamics-gate collapse.

findings.title: The frozen features partially UNDO the sparsity that hypothesis (1) worries about, but at the price of smearing the nuisance across all slots — nuisance and signal end up at comparable per-slot magnitude, so 'sparse signal starved by copy' and 'nuisance too weak' are BOTH partly wrong on this interface
findings.our: Symbolic edits are 1-of-301 (env/actions.py transition + 301-slot space in models/gate2.py:43-46). But models/gate2.py + probes/frozen_probe.py:105-144 pass each observation through ModernBERT and mean-pool per path line; the sorted-tree reordering (render.py:34) plus attention contextualization spreads a single symbolic edit across many line vectors.
findings.jepa: Hypothesis (1) predicts the target is ~copy because a discrete command changes ~1 of ~301 slots leaving the rest byte-identical — starving the dynamics signal relative to dense video where an action moves large pixel patches. 2211.10831 predicts nuisance dominates when it is denser/slower than signal.
findings.causal: Measured on the frozen interface, BOTH intuitions need qualifying. The symbolic 1-of-301 sparsity (0.357% of slots) becomes ~14.6-of-301 (4.85%) in ModernBERT feature space — the frozen encoder smears the edit, so the per-transition dynamics energy (28.8 L2) is not as copy-dominated as the symbolic view suggests, though 27.5% of transitions are still exact copies. Simultaneously the nuisance smears onto ALL ~18 present slots (3.06 each) so its total is 58% of the dynamics energy and its per-slot displacement (3.06) EXCEEDS the average per-slot genuine move (1.35, ratio 2.26). So on the interface that is the wall: the signal is NOT purely copy (feature smearing helps) and the nuisance is NOT weak (it out-displaces signal per slot). Neither the pure-sparsity story nor the weak-distractor story is the load-bearing cause; the load-bearing cause (findings 21–23) is that clean targets + path-keyed readout make the whole thing a parser, which both hypotheses are orthogonal to.
findings.redesign: If you keep the frozen-feature interface, test the distractor honestly by rendering dirty TARGETS (so the 3.06 per-slot nuisance is in the loss, not just the input) — that turns the currently-inert distractor into a real 2211.10831 stressor on this arm and would show whether SIGReg/IDM resist it here as finding 12 showed for the from-scratch arm.
findings.verify_note:
The frozen ModernBERT interface DOES partially undo the 1-of-301 symbolic sparsity that hypothesis (1) worries about: a single symbolic edit (1.073 slots, 0.357% of 301 — confirmed exactly from val.jsonl) smears to 14.60 feature slots (median 14, max 44; 4.85% of 301 — confirmed exactly from cache-val.pt). 0% of state-changing transitions are exact feature copies (the 27.5% copy fraction is entirely the no-op+invalid share, whose clean-target delta is exactly 0.000). So "the target is ~copy, dynamics signal starved" is materially softened here — this half of the finding is CONFIRMED and robust.

BUT the finding's quantitative comparison of nuisance vs signal is WRONG and directionally REVERSED. Recomputed under PYTHONHASHSEED=0 from runs/gate2/cache-val.pt (nuisance active on 100% of 5000 states, banner+noise):
- state-changing dynamics L2 = 63.64 (finding said 28.8 — NOT reproducible)
- combined nuisance L2 = 80.15 (finding said 15.91 — NOT reproducible)
- per-present-slot nuisance = 3.057 (finding 3.06 — MATCHES)
- per-changed-slot signal = 3.763; avg-per-slot signal = 4.360 (finding said 1.354 — NOT reproducible)
- per-slot ratio nuisance/signal = 3.057/3.763 = 0.81 (or /4.360 = 0.70). The finding claims 2.26 and states "nuisance out-displaces signal per slot." REVERSED: per slot, genuine signal exceeds nuisance.
- total ratio nuisance/dynamics = 80.15/63.64 = 1.26. The finding claims 0.55 (58% of energy). REVERSED: nuisance TOTAL exceeds dynamics total.
The finding's magnitudes (28.8, 15.91, 1.354) are also internally inconsistent (18x3.06=55 not 15.91; 14.6x1.354=19.8 not 28.8) and un-recoverable under any threshold/normalization I tested; actual mean present slots = 26.21, not ~18. Treat them as from an unarchived/mis-normalized computation.

Corrected reading of the two intuitions on THIS interface: (1) the sparse-copy worry is genuinely softened — 14.6 slots move, dynamics L2 is large (63.64), not near-copy. (2) The distractor is NOT weak — but because it smears broadly across ~26 present slots so its aggregate L2 (80.15) actually EXCEEDS the dynamics aggregate (63.64), NOT because it out-displaces signal per slot (per slot, signal wins 3.76 vs 3.06). So both intuitions still need qualifying, but the distractor qualification runs via breadth, not per-slot strength.

Load-bearing verdict on distractor-design: it is a valid diagnostic REFUTATION of hypothesis (2)'s framing but NOT the load-bearing cause of the negatives, and this is correct. The wall (findings 21/22/23) is measured on clean-input, clean-target, held-out layouts where the distractor is absent by construction (gate-2 targets render banner=None/noise=None, models/gate2.py:283); even a pretrained generative-LLM predictor — the right VL-JEPA model type — collapses toward copy (63.95 vs copy floor 63.13, AUC 0.803). The load-bearing cause is layout-entanglement / the symbolic-decode bottleneck at the frozen-feature interface, to which both the sparsity and distractor stories are orthogonal. The distractor is additionally not a faithful 2211.10831 analog (its slow-feature loophole is closed because targets are frozen and clean; and it is only ~3% of tokens / 6 bits per the status doc's own caveat), so it cannot be the mechanism regardless of its measured magnitude.

findings.title: Full-obs makes the prediction target a 99.6%-copy blob: a state-changing command moves 1.07 of 301 symbolic slots and leaves 96.5% of rendered lines byte-identical
findings.our: env/render.py:24 render_full serializes the ENTIRE filesystem tree + cwd after every action (the ONLY regime used in Phases 0-1; render_partial at env/render.py:43 is built but deferred to Phase 3 per terminal-jepa.md §6). The predictor operates over a fixed 301-slot key universe (models/gate2.py:45, = 258 file + 42 dir + 1 cwd). Actions are single factored (verb,arg1,arg2) tuples (env/actions.py:8) whose apply() (env/actions.py:50-190) mutates at most one file/dir/cwd slot per step.
findings.jepa: V-JEPA (2404.08471) / V-JEPA 2-AC (2506.09985) / PLDM (2502.14819) / DINO-WM all predict over DENSE, CONTINUOUS observations where an action or camera motion changes ALL or large contiguous PATCHES of the input; the prediction target after masking is a large semantic block (JEPA.md §9 recurring-finding 2: 'large semantic blocks, not random scatter'; V-JEPA multi-block masks span time). The target-encoder output moves substantially every step because the pixels do.
findings.causal: A prediction+anti-collapse objective learns whatever reduces prediction error most. When 99.6% of the target is a verbatim copy of the input, the loss-minimal solution is the identity map (copy z_t), and the tiny 1-slot delta is a rounding error the objective can ignore while still scoring near-perfectly. This is exactly the observed 'everything collapses toward a copy baseline' (finding 23): the pretrained SmolLM2-360M predictor scores 63.95 on state-changing vs the copy floor 63.13 — it does not even beat copy. The dynamics signal is not weak, it is <0.4% of the target norm, so gradient SNR for dynamics is starved by construction. CONFIRMS lead intuition (1) with numbers: our data is discrete+sparse in a way the video corpus never is.
findings.redesign: Switch the primary regime to partial-obs = prompt+stdout of the last command (render_partial). Measured on the same 200 trajs: partial-obs is 1.4 lines/obs (vs 27.4 full-obs, 20x smaller) with consecutive line-copy 0.003 (vs 0.975 full-obs) — nearly every observation is fresh, action-driven signal, so the copy baseline is no longer near-optimal and the predictor is forced to model dynamics to score.
findings.verify_note:
The DATA divergence is real and every number reproduces (mean 1.073 slots/state-changing, 96.5% byte-identical lines, copy residual 28.80 vs 0.00, copy floor 63.13). Sparsity+discreteness+full-observation is a genuine divergence from V-JEPA/PLDM/DINO-WM (dense continuous targets; masked blocks where copy is structurally impossible). It is CONTRIBUTING but NOT load-bearing as the sole cause, and the finding's causal wording needs two corrections:

(1) "the dynamics signal is <0.4% of the target norm" is FALSE. 0.4% is the slot-COUNT fraction (1.073/301=0.356%), not a norm fraction. The signal is CONCENTRATED, not drowned: Frobenius ||delta||/||target||=18.5%, per-changed-slot delta=12.5% of that slot's norm, and 99.3% of delta energy sits ON the changed slot (contextual haze on unchanged slots is only 0.71%). The "identity map is loss-minimal and the delta is a rounding error the objective ignores" story does not survive: the objective already up-weights changed slots 100x, and a per-slot signal at 12.5% of norm is not ignorable.

(2) The load-bearing variable is the REPRESENTATION and the ARCHITECTURE, not sparsity. Direct counter-evidence: tier-2 slotpred learns the IDENTICAL sparse dynamics (same 1.073-slot deltas, same 301-slot universe) to 3.9x-better-than-copy (state-chg 0.277 vs 1.076, no-op 0.000, AUC 0.906, finding 16). Sparsity cannot be the cause of collapse when the same sparsity is learnable over oracle symbolic slots. What flips learn→copy-collapse is: (a) frozen ModernBERT features require SYNTHESIZING a 768-d contextual embedding for the changed line, a generative map that fails to generalize to held-out layouts (finding 17-r2, 21, 23 — "the frozen-feature interface is the wall"); and (b) gate-2's predictor is fed the FULL previous state and computes anchor+delta with identity-at-init (gate2.py:100), so copy is the initialization and the attractor — unlike I-JEPA/V-JEPA where the target is MASKED OUT of context so copy is structurally impossible (the actual load-bearing JEPA divergence, per JEPA.md §3/§9-finding-2). Reframe the finding as: "full-observation + a copy-available predictor architecture (anchor+delta) over a target where 96.5% of lines are byte-identical makes copy a near-optimal, identity-at-init attractor — but the sparse delta itself is learnable (tier-2 proves it); the collapse is driven by the frozen-continuous-feature synthesis burden failing to transfer across layouts, not by target sparsity starving SNR."

On the distractor (intuition 2): valid-but-underpowered analog of 2211.10831 (1 line, 6 bits, per-trajectory-fixed = correct slow-feature construction), already conceded weak in the project's own audit and already shown to bite recipe-dependently (finding 12). For the DYNAMICS-gate negatives specifically it is a red herring: targets render CLEAN, so the distractor's contribution to the scored target space is exactly 0.00 (verified) — it cannot cause the gate-2 collapse.

findings.title: Full-obs is a machine-readable serialization of the state, so there is nothing to abstract — the exact opposite of what JEPA's latent target buys you, and why a random-init encoder ties the pretrained one
findings.our: render_full (env/render.py:24) emits 'cwd: <path>' then a sorted 'tree:' listing of every dir ('a/b/') and file with its content class ('b/notes.txt [c3]'). env/parse.py:15 parse_full is a trivial deterministic inverse (round-trip tested). The A1 path-keyed readout (probes/frozen_probe.py, finding 14) mean-pools each rendered LINE and reads its symbols off — it 'is the parser' (finding 14: exact over 25k states, 0 mismatches).
findings.jepa: JEPA's entire premise (JEPA.md §1, §3.2) is that the target encoder DISCARDS unpredictable/irrelevant detail so the model spends capacity on predictable semantic structure; the abstraction only pays off when the raw observation contains high-bandwidth nuisance the encoder must strip (grass texture, pond ripples). JEPA.md §3.2 explicitly caveats that reconstruction 'works well for text — tokens are discrete and low-bandwidth, so the unpredictable-detail problem is manageable'. terminal-jepa.md §4 concedes: 'in clean full-obs the observation IS a machine-readable serialization of the probe targets, so the reconstruction twin sits near the probing ceiling by construction.'
findings.causal: Full-obs collapses the observation onto the state one-to-one and losslessly. There is zero irrelevant detail to abstract away, so JEPA's differentiating mechanism is inert — which is precisely why (finding 22) a RANDOM-INIT ModernBERT decodes held-out content at 100% and cwd at 83.9% under the path-keyed readout, closing the pretraining margin to +5.35 and failing the +20 gate. When the observation already IS the answer written in plain text, contextualized token features trivially expose it and JEPA training can only make linear decodability WORSE (finding 13: trained-below-untrained). The task gives JEPA nothing to do, so it cannot demonstrate value. This is a full-obs artifact, independent of sparsity: even a dense observation that were a lossless serialization would defeat the abstraction premise.
findings.redesign: Realism forces the observation to be a LOSSY, PARTIAL projection of state (stdout is a query result, truncated, noisy), restoring the gap between observation and state that abstraction is supposed to close. Partial-obs (ls shows one dir, cat one file) means the encoder must integrate a stream of partial views into a latent belief — a genuine representation task with detail to abstract, matching JEPA's operating regime rather than defeating it.
findings.verify_note:
CONFIRMED for gate 1, REFINED for the broader claim. The divergence "full-obs is a lossless serialization, so there is nothing to abstract" is real and load-bearing for the SPECIFIC negative it targets: the representation gate (gate 1), where a random-init ModernBERT ties the pretrained encoder. Recomputed from the committed artifacts (runs/frozen-modernbert-v1/probe.json and probe-randominit.json): random-init path-keyed content-given-existence = 100.0% (identical to pretrained), cwd 83.9% vs 100%, macro margin +5.35 < +20; pooled +13.77 < +20 — both gate-1 clauses fail. The mechanism is verified at the token level: the content-class tag [cK] is literally inside the file line the path-keyed head pools (probes/frozen_probe.py key_lines/content_pairs), so a random projection linearly exposes it — the observation IS the answer in plain text, exactly JEPA.md §3.2's low-bandwidth-discrete-text caveat, and the recon-twin parity (whose entire pooled edge is the cwd scalar; content 0.117 BELOW the 0.175 majority floor per runs/recon-v1-s0-4k/probe-v2.json) confirms §3.2 rather than crediting JEPA.

CORRECTION to scope: the clause "This is a full-obs artifact, independent of sparsity" over-reaches when the finding is used to explain the whole project's negatives, especially the dynamics gate (findings 21/23). Those failures have a distinct, co-load-bearing mechanism I measured from runs/gate2/cache-val.pt: (1) symbolic sparsity — a state-changing transition changes mean 1.078 of 301 slots (0.358%; no-ops/invalids change exactly 0), so the prediction target is near-copy (feature copy floor 63.6) and the dynamics signal is genuinely starved; and (2) ModernBERT contextual spill — even a predictor that perfectly synthesizes the one changed line still eats 56.7% of the copy-floor error (35.1 of 63.6) reproducing perturbations on 26 textually-unchanged lines on held-out layouts. Sparsity is therefore NOT separable from the dynamics negatives; it is a second load-bearing divergence entangled with the lossless-serialization one. Net: keep the finding as stated for gate 1 (confirmed there); scope it explicitly to the representation gate; do NOT let it absorb the dynamics-gate negatives, which the project lead's intuition-(1) sparsity finding co-explains. Files: /Users/fanzhu/PyCharmProjects/jepa/terminal-jepa/env/render.py:24, env/parse.py:15-39, probes/frozen_probe.py:80-200, models/gate2.py, evals/dynamics.py:76-92; artifacts runs/frozen-modernbert-v1/probe.json, probe-randominit.json, runs/recon-v1-s0-4k/probe-v2.json, runs/gate2/cache-val.pt.

findings.title: Serializing the whole tree turns a 1-line header nuisance into contamination of ~25 of 301 slots via bidirectional contextualization — this manufactures the 'diffuse haze' the predictors keep failing on
findings.our: The dynamic-noise header (env/render.py:10-12, a per-step '[ts ...] [pid ...]' line) and per-trajectory banner (env/render.py:15-21) are prepended to the FULL 27-line tree render, then the whole thing goes through bidirectional ModernBERT (models/gate2.py precompute). Because attention is full/bidirectional, the resampled header token features leak into every slot's contextual vector. The A1 predictor targets those 301x768 contextual vectors.
findings.jepa: In V-JEPA/PLDM the observation is spatially local: a distractor in one image region contaminates only nearby patch tokens, and JEPA.md §5 ('JEPAs Focus on Slow Features', 2211.10831) frames the canonical failure as a distractor that is SLOWER than the signal getting copied — a per-step-RESAMPLED header is a FAST distractor, which that paper says is the benign case. The papers never feed a full global serialization through a single bidirectional pass where a 1-line nuisance can reach all tokens.
findings.causal: On no-op/invalid transitions the TRUE state delta is exactly 0, yet in the deployed dirty regime the per-step header resample moves ~24.6 of 301 slot vectors, giving a spurious copy residual of ~4.5. This is 16% of a real state-change magnitude (28.8) and it fires EVERY step, so the 'copy' target the predictor must reproduce is itself noisy in the untouched slots. This is exactly the 'no-op/invalid haze' that fails the calibration clauses in findings 17, 21, 23 (round-5 no-op 1.65, invalid 2.63, AUC 0.803 < 0.9). It is a full-obs + global-bidirectional-encoding artifact: the same header on a 1-line partial obs would contaminate ~1 slot, not 25. Note this is a SECONDARY amplifier, not the root cause — the copy-collapse (finding 1) fails even in the CLEAN regime where haze is 0.
findings.redesign: A realistic partial-obs stream keeps each observation to ~1 stdout line, so a header nuisance can only contaminate a bounded local context, not the entire state. Alternatively, if full-obs is kept, the header should not be a global prefix over a bidirectional encoder — but partial-obs removes the pathology structurally.
findings.verify_note: Serializing the whole tree does spread a 1-line header nuisance across ~25 of 301 slots via bidirectional ModernBERT — this is real and reproduces on cache-val.pt (no-op 23.2 / invalid 25.7 slots touched, ~24.6 mean; clean-regime residual exactly 0.00; top-slot holds 84.4% of state-change L2 energy, ~16% spillover across 26.3 slots). BUT this header contamination is NOT causally load-bearing for the negatives it is blamed for. The scored failing batteries (finding 21 round-4, finding 23 round-5: no-op 1.65, invalid 2.63, AUC 0.803) all ran --input-regime CLEAN (adapter "gate2-clean"), so the input carries zero header noise; the prediction target is clean by construction and identical across all 1320 no-op/invalid transitions (max diff 0.00e+00), so it is not "noisy in untouched slots"; and the calibration clause is self-referential ||predict(z,a)−z|| (dynamics.py:175) where the copy floor scores exactly 0.00 — the genllm's 1.65/2.63 is predictor-hallucinated change on a header-free clean input, which header haze cannot produce. The finding's own caveat that copy-collapse fails in the clean regime (haze=0) is the tell: the deployed/scored regime IS clean. The ONE surviving grain of truth — the 16% bidirectional-contextualization spillover into 26 symbolically-unchanged slots — is a property of the ModernBERT TARGET representation (present even in clean targets), NOT of the per-step header, and is already the subject of finding 17's diffuse-haze diagnosis; it inflates the state-changing target's entanglement but does not touch the no-op/invalid calibration failures at all. Reclassify: header/global-serialization contamination is a red herring for the negatives; target-space contextualization spread is a separate, weaker contributor already owned by finding 17. Also correct the slow-vs-fast conflation: the per-step noise line is the FAST distractor (2211.10831's benign case, correctly noted), but the project's intended slow-feature distractor is the per-trajectory BANNER (terminal-jepa.md:27), a different object.

findings.title: Partial-obs is more JEPA-aligned in action-coupling but is NOT a free win — it swaps the copy-collapse problem for a partial-observability/memory problem the project deliberately deferred (W=1)
findings.our: terminal-jepa.md §4 fixes predictor context window W=1 for Phase 1 ('partial-obs configurations — longer W, memory — live in Phase 3'). The plan's own §6 states plainly: 'nothing in this design maintains an explicit belief state over unobserved files.' So the current architecture cannot consume the partial-obs stream that would fix findings 1-3.
findings.jepa: V-JEPA 2-AC and PLDM assume FULL observability at the representation level (terminal-jepa.md §1: 'PLDM explicitly assumes full observability'; JEPA.md §5). Their single-frame (or short-clip) observation already determines the relevant state; they do not solve long-horizon partial observability / belief tracking — that is listed as an open problem (JEPA.md §10: 'goal specification requires goal images', short planning horizons). So partial-obs is realistic for a terminal AGENT but is a HARDER setting than anything the JEPA planning corpus demonstrates.
findings.causal: This refines, not just confirms, lead intuition (1). The realism-fix (partial-obs) that dissolves the copy-collapse and the trivial-parsing problems simultaneously INTRODUCES the memory problem: a single stdout line (mean 1.4 lines, 84-100% action-dependent for ls/cat) does not determine the full state, so a W=1 predictor over partial obs would be predicting from an under-determined context and could legitimately fail for a reason unrelated to JEPA. The honest framing: full-obs was chosen to 'isolate the representation question from the memory question' (terminal-jepa.md §3) — but that isolation is what made the representation question TRIVIAL (finding 2) and the dynamics target a copy (finding 1). The two intuitions and the deferral interact: you cannot get JEPA-realistic observations without also taking on the memory problem, so a redesign must budget for W>1 / recurrent belief, not just flip the render flag.
findings.redesign: Adopt the plan's own Phase-3 goal protocol as the Phase-1 setting: (a) partial-obs stream + longer/recurrent context (W in {3,5} or GRU state) so the predictor accumulates a belief; (b) goals become 'expected stdout of a diagnostic command' (cat b/notes.txt shows [c3]) rather than a rendered full state, since many states render identically under partial-obs. This is the smallest change that makes observations JEPA-aligned (dense, action-coupled, lossy) while keeping ground-truth probing — but it must be scoped as its own hard problem, not a drop-in for full-obs.
findings.verify_note:
FINDING (refines-hypothesis; a real divergence, but subordinate to layout-entanglement): Partial-obs is genuinely more JEPA-aligned in action->observation coupling, but it is NOT a free win — it trades full-obs copy-collapse for a partial-observability/memory problem the project deliberately deferred (W=1), and, critically, it does NOT touch the load-bearing negative (non-transferable absolute-state representation / layout-entanglement).

REPRODUCED NUMBERS (PYTHONHASHSEED=0, data/v1/val.jsonl 9,600 transitions; runs/gate2/cache-val.pt 4,800 transitions):
- Symbolic sparsity: state-changing touches mean 1.073/301 slots (median 1, max 2); 27.5% of transitions change 0 slots; mean slots touched = 0.26% of the 301-slot universe.
- Partial-obs stdout action-and-state-dependent: ls 17.1 chars/84.2% nonempty, cat 18.7/100%, cd/mv 0/0%. Consecutive partial obs share 0.3% of lines (mean Jaccard 0.0030).
- Action-coupling contrast (the finding's strongest point): full-obs consecutive observations are 96.6% line-identical vs partial-obs 0.3% — full-obs is 324x more copy-like. Partial-obs matches the PLDM/V-JEPA-2-AC dense-coupling regime.
- Memory problem quantified: a single partial obs determines mean ~1.2/301 slots (0.4% of state); full-obs serializes ~27.5 lines = whole state. A W=1 predictor over one partial obs is under-determined.
- Architecture pin verified: no code path uses render_partial (grep); predictor is (z_t,action)->z_{t+1}, W=1 (models/nets.py:86). Citations verified verbatim: terminal-jepa.md section 4:42 (W=1, "partial-obs ... live in Phase 3"), section 6:69 ("nothing in this design maintains an explicit belief state over unobserved files"), section 3:25 ("isolates the representation question from the memory question"), section 1:9 ("PLDM explicitly assumes full observability").

KEY REFINEMENT (challenges lead intuition 1 and the finding's own "swaps copy-collapse" framing): copy-collapse is a SYMPTOM, not the disease. In full-obs feature space the copy floor PERFECTLY separates transition types (state-changing total-L2 63.6 vs no-op/invalid exactly 0.00 — a trivial threshold gives AUC~1.0), so the sparse/copy target does not "starve the dynamics signal"; the predictor's AUC-0.803 failure is inability to SYNTHESIZE the changed 768-d vector on held-out layouts. On SEEN layouts dynamics are learned near-perfectly (finding 16: 78.7% exact-match, rollout medians 0). The load-bearing negative across findings 3/10/12/13/21/23 is layout-entanglement — the representation memorizes layout instances and decodes held-out layouts worse than random features (finding 13); finding 23 shows even a dense-target pretrained generative LLM "collapses toward copy on held-out layouts, the same layout-entanglement." Partial-obs does not fix this. So a redesign must budget for BOTH (a) W>1 / recurrent belief tracking to consume the partial-obs stream AND (b) a mechanism that forces a path-consistent, transferable absolute-state code (A2/A3, augmentation-invariance, or the frozen-feature symbolic-decode interface) — because partial-obs alone fixes action-coupling while leaving the primary transfer bottleneck untouched.

findings.title: 94% of the gate-2 prediction target is a copy of the input — the forward-dynamics objective has almost no learnable signal, the opposite of V-JEPA masked prediction where the copyable fraction is ~0
findings.our: Gate 2 trains a predictor to map frozen ModernBERT features of state_t + action to frozen features of state_{t+1} (models/gate2.py:91-101 Gate2Net.forward; loss = per-slot L2 to the clean next-feature map, gate2.py:406-413). The target[t+1] is the SAME kind of object as the input[t] (a full 301x768 slot map), and identity-at-init is enforced (gate2.py:82-83, 375-382) so the net literally starts as a copy. evals/dynamics.py CopyDynamics (predict=identity) is the pre-registered floor.
findings.jepa: V-JEPA / I-JEPA (JEPA.md §3, §9 finding 2): the target is the EMBEDDING OF MASKED-OUT patches/tubelets absent from the context encoder's input. The predictor cannot copy any visible token into a masked slot — every target token must be synthesized from context + positional mask token. Masking is 'the only corruption' (JEPA.md:47) and large-semantic-block masking is load-bearing (72.9 vs 51.5 K400 for multi-block vs random-tube, JEPA.md:126). The learnable fraction of the target is ~100%.
findings.causal: Recomputed on runs/gate2/cache-val.pt: mean ||target next-feature-map|| = 797.5; mean ||target - input|| (residual the predictor must add) = 46.14, so 94.2% of the target magnitude is literally the input carried forward and only 5.8% is residual. A copy already achieves 94% target-magnitude match, so the gradient toward 'learn dynamics' vs 'stay a copy' is a 6% signal buried under a 94% copy attractor. This is why every gate-2 arm 'collapses toward copy' (findings 17, 21, 23) and why AUC sits at 0.78-0.80 < 0.9 — the objective barely distinguishes a good dynamics model from identity. Masked-prediction papers never face this: their target is un-copyable by construction, so the objective's entire signal IS the thing we want learned.
findings.redesign: Two options restore an un-copyable target: (a) adopt MASKED prediction as the pretext (Track A2): mask the slot(s) an action will touch and predict them from the rest + action, so identity is unavailable for the target region — learnable fraction ~100% on masked slots instead of 6% globally. (b) Make the target the DELTA/edit (which slot changed + new value) and score only changed+neighbor slots, so copy scores 0 not 94%.
findings.verify_note:
CONTRIBUTING, not load-bearing. The descriptive statistic is exactly reproduced (cache-val.pt: mean ||target|| 797.54, residual 46.14, copy-fraction 0.9422, state-changing floor 63.64≈63.13, no-op/invalid 0.0000) and intuition (1) holds at the text/symbolic level (env/render.py:24-35: one command edits ~1 of ~15 file lines). But three recomputed counter-checks refute the CAUSAL claim that the 94% copy attractor starves the dynamics gradient and causes the negatives:

1. Feature-space is not ~copy: a one-line edit spreads across ~14.6 of ~27 present slots (mean dz 3.76) because ModernBERT is contextual. The 94% aggregate is inflated by ~274 always-zero absent slots and by averaging in no-op/invalid (residual exactly 0). The code already edit-weights changed slots 11x (gate2.py:411-413), amplifying the signal 6.3x; finding 21 shows this does not rescue the gate.

2. Seen-vs-held-out dissociation (the decisive point the FINDING omits): the same-layout train monitor (gate2.py:363-365) reaches state-changing copy_ratio 0.825 — the predictor beats copy by 17.5% IN-DISTRIBUTION (runs/gate2/round5_chain.out). A gradient-starving copy attractor cannot coexist with beating copy on seen layouts. The failure appears only on held-out LAYOUTS (63.95 > 63.13), i.e. it is the layout-transfer wall (findings 3/10/13/21/23), which is invariant to copy-fraction.

3. The gate fails mostly on ANTI-copy clauses: no-op=1.65 and invalid=2.63 (copy floor 0.0) — the net adds haze exactly where it should copy, the opposite of collapsing to copy; ew0->ew10 worsens this monotonically.

Reference-mechanism correction: the comparison to I-JEPA/V-JEPA MASKED prediction (copy-fraction ~0) is the wrong reference. Gate-2 is an action-conditioned FORWARD-DYNAMICS predictor (terminal-jepa.md §4/§10 cite V-JEPA-2-AC / DINO-WM / PLDM / 2512.24497). In THAT sub-family consecutive-frame embeddings are also highly correlated, so a copy baseline is strong there too — the copy attractor is a general, known property of JEPA world models (JEPA.md §5/§9-4 make multi-step rollout the load-bearing countermeasure; the slow-features paper §5/§10 warns the slow distractor gets copied). So 'masked-prediction papers never face this' compares against a sub-family terminal-jepa is not replicating.

Load-bearing cause of the negatives (where all 23 findings converge): held-out-layout representation transfer — the frozen contextual features are content-complete but not layout-canonical (findings 13/14/22), and no learned predictor over them transfers (findings 21/23 conclude 'the frozen-feature interface is the wall, not the predictor'). Remediation implication: attacking copy-fraction (predict deltas / mask slots / un-copyable targets) would target a symptom; the in-distribution monitor already learns dynamics fine. The real lever is a transferable/canonical state code (e.g. the symbolic-decode bottleneck that finding 17-round-3 shows works), consistent with the project's own converged diagnosis.

findings.title: Discrete-sparse dynamics make the target ~copy: 0.78 of 301 slots change per transition and 27.5% of transitions change nothing — per-transition target density orders of magnitude below the dense video/robot data the recipe was tuned on
findings.our: env/actions.py transitions change at most a handful of symbolic slots: a command edits ~1 of 301 path/content/cwd slots and leaves the rest byte-identical (render.render_full serializes the whole tree; env/render.py:24-35). Invalid (15%) and valid-no-op (12%) actions change nothing (actions.py:62-64 _finish returns VALID_NO_OP when new==old; INVALID never mutates).
findings.jepa: V-JEPA video (JEPA.md:75), PLDM/DINO-WM/V-JEPA2-AC images (JEPA.md:85-87): an action or camera motion changes ALL or large PATCHES of pixels — dense, continuous per-frame change across the whole grid. The recipe (rollout training vs compounding error, AdaLN conditioning, CEM planning) was validated where every step moves most of the observation. JEPA.md:129 'a small amount of interaction data goes a long way' assumes each interaction is information-dense.
findings.causal: Recomputed on data/v1/val.jsonl (9600 transitions): mean 0.78 of 301 slots change per transition (0.26% of the slot universe); 27.5% change 0 slots; state-changing (72.5%) change a MEDIAN of 1 slot, MAX 2, only 2.9% of PRESENT slots. The ground-truth target is dominated by 'nothing changed' or 'one slot changed', which copy nails. Confirmed in feature space: copy is EXACTLY optimal (delta=0.000) on 27.5% of transitions because clean renders are byte-identical -> ModernBERT emits bit-identical features. The model that predicts 'copy' is right 27.5% of the time for free and ~94% right in magnitude always (finding above). This directly produces the no-op/invalid 'haze' the battery flags (copy scores 0 there, learned predictors 5-17).
findings.redesign: Increase per-transition target density so copy stops being near-optimal: (a) make actions COMPOUND (one 'action' = a short script touching many slots), (b) add cascading dynamics (cwd-relative renders, propagating content, quotas) so one command legitimately changes many slots, or (c) drop the 27% no-op/invalid mass from the PREDICTION objective (keep it in a separate calibration head) so the dynamics loss isn't 27% trivially-solved-by-copy.
findings.verify_note:
The divergence is REAL and the numbers are accurate (all reproduced to 2-3 sig figs), but it is CONTRIBUTING, not load-bearing, for the headline negatives. Verdict: the discrete-sparse target density amplifies the copy baseline and creates the calibration failure surface, but it is NOT the root cause of the transfer negatives.

REPRODUCED EXACTLY (PYTHONHASHSEED=0, data/v1):
- Symbolic (val.jsonl, 9600 transitions): 0.778 mean slots changed / 301 (0.258% of universe); 27.53% zero-change; state-changing median 1 / max 2, 92.67% change exactly 1, 7.33% change 2; no-op (1186) and invalid (1457) change EXACTLY 0 slots (confirms env/actions.py:62-64 and INVALID-never-mutates). The "2.9% of present slots" figure is the ratio-of-means (2.99%); mean-of-ratios is 3.23% — both correct, the finding used the former.
- Feature space (runs/gate2/cache-val.pt, 4800 transitions): 27.50% have CLEAN feature-delta EXACTLY 0.000, bijective with the no-op+invalid set (1320 = 604+716), zero leakage; copy 94.0% right in magnitude aggregate (91.7% on state-changing). State-changing clean delta mean 63.6 = the round-4/5 copy floor 63.13.
- All round-4/5 learned-predictor arms fail every calibration clause (noop 1.65-12, inv 2.63-22 vs copy 0; AUC 0.78-0.88 < 0.9; state-chg 63.95-72.18 all > 63.13 copy floor). The "haze" claim (copy 0, learned 5-17) reproduces.

DECISIVE COUNTER-EVIDENCE (why sparsity is not the load-bearing cause):
1. The IDENTICAL sparse dynamics under an ORACLE/symbolic representation (tier-2 slotpred-v2, runs/tier2/slotpred-v2/train_log.json step 3000) are learned near-perfectly: 78.7% exact-match on state-changing, 100% on no-op, 81% on invalid. If 0.78-slots/transition sparsity starved the dynamics signal, this predictor facing the same sparsity would fail too — it doesn't. Status finding 16 says exactly this: the architecture CAN learn the dynamics near-perfectly when representation isn't the problem.
2. The failing frozen-feature arms DO learn the sparse dynamics on SEEN layouts (copy-ratio 0.83-0.85 on state-changing, round4/round5 train monitors) and collapse toward copy only on HELD-OUT layouts. The wall is the frozen-feature-over-held-out-layouts interface (findings 13/21/23), not target density.
3. The "copy is exactly optimal on 27.5%" property is a CONSEQUENCE OF THE CLEAN-TARGET DECISION (gate2.py:8-9, finding 15a), not an intrinsic domain property: in the dirty/deployed regime the no-op/invalid feature-delta is mean ~19-20 (per-step noise line), not zero. So the zero-target property that makes copy unbeatable was engineered, not forced by the data.

WHERE SPARSITY GENUINELY CONTRIBUTES (why not a red herring): (a) it removes the dense reconstruction pressure the JEPA recipe was tuned on (JEPA.md:16, 61-66, 125: V-JEPA +5.1 latent-vs-pixel, physics +51% all assume dense continuous targets), making the from-scratch prediction+anti-collapse objective trivially satisfiable by ~copy — matching the status doc's own "prediction+anti-collapse neither pays for nor identifies an absolute-state code"; (b) the 27.5% exact-zero-target mass makes copy a stiff, calibration-sensitive competitor and IS the clause learned predictors keep failing; (c) in the 301x768 L2 metric the 1-changed-slot signal is a ~8% relative perturbation drowned by ~300 unchanged slots — a needle that is hard precisely because of sparsity.

CORRECT ATTRIBUTION: the divergence from V-JEPA/PLDM/DINO-WM/V-JEPA2-AC dense/continuous data (JEPA.md:75, 85-87) is real and correctly measured, but its causal role is to make the copy baseline strong and the calibration surface unforgiving — an amplifier — while the load-bearing cause of the transfer negatives is representation/held-out-layout entanglement, isolated by the oracle-representation control that learns the same sparse dynamics near-perfectly. The finding overclaims "orders of magnitude below -> CAUSES these negatives"; the accurate statement is "makes ~copy a strong competitor and the no-op/invalid calibration clause the primary failing surface, on top of a representation-transfer failure that persists independently."

findings.title: ModernBERT contextualization smears a 1-slot edit across ~15 slots, and 57% of the feature-space target-delta lands on slots whose symbolic value did NOT change — the frozen-feature interface manufactures un-semantic spillover the predictor is scored on
findings.our: Gate 2's target is the frozen bidirectional ModernBERT per-line feature map of the clean next observation (gate2.py precompute:283-287, path-keyed line vectors). Because ModernBERT is bidirectional over the whole sorted tree, changing one line's text perturbs the contextual vector of many other (textually unchanged) lines. The predictor is scored by L2 over all 301 slots (dynamics.py distance sums per-slot norms, gate2.py:604-605).
findings.jepa: This is the exact failure V-JEPA 2.1 diagnosed and fixed (JEPA.md:78, 126): 'supervising the predictor only on masked tokens destroys local spatial structure' -> noisy dense per-patch features; fix = supervise all tokens with distance-weighted loss + deep supervision. VL-JEPA (JEPA.md:110) pairs a frozen text encoder target with a ~490M-1B predictor precisely because synthesizing contextual text-semantic embeddings is a heavy generative burden. The JEPA papers treat frozen-target contextual spillover as a first-class problem requiring dense-all-token supervision or a large predictor.
findings.causal: Recomputed on cache-val.pt state-changing transitions: the single symbolic edit propagates to a mean 14.6 slots moving >1.0 in feature space; of 63.6 L2 units of target-delta, only 28.5 (43.3%) sit on the symbolically-changed slot(s) and 35.1 (56.7%) is spillover onto UNCHANGED slots. To lower L2 the predictor must reproduce this 56.7% of un-semantic, context-dependent drift with no symbolic handle — a generative burden our 0.64M-316M predictors cannot carry (finding 17 'must synthesize 768-d contextual embeddings ... cf VL-JEPA's 490M predictor'). This is the mechanistic root of the 'diffuse haze' in findings 17/21/23 and why pretraining/scale help only marginally (finding 23): the interface injects noise that is not learnable dynamics.
findings.redesign: Either (a) score the predictor only on symbolically-changed slots plus a small neighborhood (V-JEPA-2.1 distance-weighted supervision), removing the 57% un-semantic spillover from the loss, or (b) use a target encoder with LOCAL/path-scoped context (per-line encoding without cross-line attention, or a slot-autoencoder bottleneck) so a 1-slot edit yields a ~1-slot target-delta — restoring symbolic sparsity into feature space. The codebook pipeline (finding 17) works precisely because its linear decoder discards this spillover.
findings.verify_note: The divergence is REAL and reproducible (all numbers confirmed to within noise from cache-val.pt: 14.6 slots move >1.0; target-delta L2 63.6 splits 28.5 signal / 35.1 spillover ≈ 45%/55%; contamination 79.6/26.0 slots), and it is a genuine structural difference from the JEPA reference setups: a single symbolic action produces a DENSE, bidirectionally-contextualized 768-d-per-line target where the JEPA papers either supervise such dense targets with all-token loss + deep supervision (V-JEPA 2.1) or synthesize text-semantic embeddings with a ~490M-1B predictor (VL-JEPA). But the finding OVERSTATES the causal story in one specific, load-bearing way: the spillover is NOT 'un-semantic noise that is not learnable dynamics.' It is a fully DETERMINISTIC function of the symbolic state (recurring states → byte-identical features, max diff 0.0000) and the predictor demonstrably LEARNS it in-distribution (seen-layout copy_ratio 0.825). The held-out negatives are driven by COMPOSITIONAL TRANSFER FAILURE, not by spillover-as-noise: on held-out layouts the predictor scores WORSE than copy (63.95 > 63.13), failing even the signal slot, while a LINEAR decoder on the identical frozen features reads state at ~100% on held-out (finding 17) — proving the features are transfer-able for reading. So the correct verdict is CONTRIBUTING: the contextual spillover is a real, deterministic dense-synthesis burden that raises the copy floor and forces the predictor to regenerate ~15 per-line embeddings rather than flip one symbolic bit (this is why gate-2 caps at ratio 0.825 even in-distribution and adds no-op haze), which sharpens and compounds the negatives — but it is NOT 'the mechanistic root.' The root is the project's standing layout-entanglement problem operating at the DYNAMICS level in feature space; the frozen-feature target-spillover is a genuine aggravating factor and a valid recipe-fidelity divergence, not the sole cause. Recommended reframing for the status doc: 'the frozen bidirectional-encoder target imposes a deterministic-but-dense contextual-synthesis burden (55% of target-delta is off the changed slot) that the predictor learns in-distribution but cannot compositionally transfer; combined with the linear-decode-transfers / dynamics-doesn't dissociation, the frozen-feature interface is a load-bearing contributor to, not the manufacturer of, the held-out failure.'

findings.title: Factored-discrete action space over an ~837k combinatorial arg universe is a genuine mismatch to every JEPA planner (all sample 2-7 dim continuous) — but it bites PLANNING/CEM, not the representation/dynamics negatives, so it is not the cause of the current gate failures
findings.our: Actions are factored discrete (verb, arg1, arg2) with verb in 9, args over the 301-path + 8-content vocabulary (env/actions.py:8, vocab.py); write alone spans 258x8=2064, cp/mv 258x258=66564, total factored space ~837,081. plan/cem.py runs CEM/random-shooting over this discrete space; finding 18 measured the two-arg 'needle' at P~1/865k per unfiltered draw and CEM adding ~nothing over random shooting.
