findings.jepa: Every JEPA planner samples LOW-DIM CONTINUOUS actions and relies on distribution-sharpening: V-JEPA2-AC 7-dim end-effector deltas via CEM (JEPA.md:86), PLDM 2-dim velocity via MPPI on PointMaze (JEPA.md:85), DINO-WM/2512.24497 low-dim continuous, IWM continuous transform params (JEPA.md:84). PLDM 'explicitly assumes full observability' AND continuous actions (terminal-jepa.md:9). CEM beats gradient planning 'on non-smooth landscapes' (JEPA.md:87) but still needs a metric action space to interpolate elites — a 3-way categorical over 300 paths has no gradient to sharpen (finding 18: 'distribution-sharpening has nothing to grip when improvement is needle-shaped').
findings.causal: This IS a real reference-fidelity divergence and it plausibly makes RQ3's +30-over-random CEM bar unreachable (finding 18). BUT it operates at the PLANNING layer (gate 3), downstream of the representation/dynamics failures (gates 1-2) that are the current wall. The gate-2 negatives (findings 21, 23: predictor collapses to copy on held-out layouts) are caused by the target-density/copy problem (findings above), which would occur under ANY action space including a continuous one. So the factored-discrete action space is load-bearing for the PLANNER but is NOT the cause of the observed representation/dynamics negatives — it becomes the binding constraint only after gates 1-2 pass. Challenging the lead's framing: it is a core mismatch for the END GOAL (planning), but the intuition that it explains the current negatives is unsupported — the copy-attractor does.
findings.redesign: For a JEPA-faithful PLANNING setup: (a) shrink the arg space drastically (small tree so the action set is O(10s) like a discretized continuous grid), (b) commit to Planner B (LLM-propose / world-model-rank, plan §5) as the only viable planner in the combinatorial regime and drop the CEM +30 bar for full-obs, or (c) embed actions in a continuous space with a learned decoder so CEM has a metric to sharpen. None fixes gates 1-2; they matter only once the copy-attractor is resolved.
findings.verify_note:
FINDING (confirmed, with a sharpened causal boundary): The factored-discrete action space over an ~837,081 combinatorial universe (recomputed exactly: 9 verbs × 301 arg1 × 309 arg2; write 2064, cp/mv 66564; needle P = 1/837,081) is a genuine reference-fidelity mismatch to every JEPA planner (all sample 2–7 dim CONTINUOUS actions: V-JEPA2-AC 7-dim, PLDM 2-dim, DINO-WM low-dim — JEPA.md:85-87). It bites PLANNING/CEM (gate 3), NOT the representation/dynamics negatives (gates 1-2), so it is not the cause of the current gate failures.

Evidence recomputed and confirmed: CEM 27% vs random 23% (+4), filtered 73% vs 76% (−3), scripted 100% — exact against runs/tier1/cem-oracle.json; val predicate skew 436/534 = 81.6% file_exists_with_class (data/v1/manifest.json); the +30-over-random RQ3 bar (finding 18) is unreachable by CEM here because improvement is needle-shaped (P≈1/837k).

Why it is NOT the cause of the gate-1/2 negatives (three lines; two newly recomputed):
1. The dynamics/representation evals never search actions. evals/dynamics.py feeds actions FROM the data; models/gate2.py:599 predict() maps ONE (verb,arg1,arg2) → ONE next-latent. The 837k size enters only when plan/cem.py searches. Gate-1/2 action-search space = 0.
2. The gate-2 negatives are the copy-attractor, driven by target DENSITY, not the action space. Recomputed from runs/gate2/cache-val.pt with the exact eval metric: state-changing feature move = 63.6 (= the finding's 63.13 copy floor), no-op/invalid = 0.000 exactly. From data/v1/val.jsonl: 92.7% of state-changing transitions = 1 symbolic edit (0.36% of ~301 slots), 27.6% of all transitions = 0 edits. Findings 21/23 confirm every learned arm sits at/above copy (63.95–72.18) with residual no-op/invalid haze. This collapse would occur under ANY action space, continuous included.
3. NEW: the huge space is not even an action-coverage bottleneck for the predictor. Predictors embed actions FACTORED (gate2.py:97-98). Despite only 1.28% combinatorial coverage in train, factored-atom coverage is verbs 9/9, arg1 96.8%, arg2 85.9%, union 99.7% — the learner sees every atom hundreds of times.

Net: load-bearing for the PLANNER (the end goal), a red herring for the observed representation/dynamics negatives. The lead's intuition that the action space explains the current wall is unsupported; the copy-attractor / frozen-feature interface (finding 23) is the wall. Relevant files: /Users/fanzhu/PyCharmProjects/jepa/terminal-jepa/env/vocab.py, env/actions.py, plan/cem.py, evals/dynamics.py, models/gate2.py, models/tier2.py, runs/tier1/cem-oracle.json, runs/gate2/cache-val.pt, data/v1/val.jsonl, data/v1/manifest.json; /Users/fanzhu/PyCharmProjects/jepa/JEPA.md (lines 85-87); /Users/fanzhu/PyCharmProjects/jepa/terminal-jepa.md (lines 9,59,23); /Users/fanzhu/PyCharmProjects/jepa/terminal-jepa-status.md (findings 18,21,23).

findings.title: The per-trajectory banner distractor IS a valid slow-feature analog and in feature space is STRONGER than signal (contamination 80 L2 > signal 64 L2) — but it does not cause the gate failures, and 'trivially predictable' understates it
findings.our: terminal-jepa.md:27 designs a per-trajectory banner (64 banners, ~6 bits, fixed within trajectory) + per-step dynamic noise, claimed to be 'the slow-feature trap from 2211.10831' and 'trivially predictable in time'. render.py:15-21 prepends 1 banner line + 1 dynamic-noise line. Status caveat (line 132) already concedes it is 'a far weaker trap than 2211.10831's full-frame distractor (one line, ~3% of tokens, 6 bits vs image-scale noise)'.
findings.jepa: 2211.10831 (JEPA.md:94, §10): JEPA implements slow-feature learning; the failure is a distractor SLOWER than the signal — a fixed noise BACKGROUND (large fraction of the frame) copied into the representation, satisfying the objective without learning dynamics. Their failing recipes were VICReg/SimCLR-based; the distractor is dense (frame-scale) and its danger is that variance-regularizers reward copying it.
findings.causal: Two-sided verdict. CONFIRMS the analog is valid and even understated: recomputed dirty-vs-clean feature contamination = 80.15 L2 over 26.2 slots, LARGER than genuine state-change 63.6 L2 over 14.6 slots (ratio 1.26). Because ModernBERT is bidirectional, the banner+noise lines perturb every slot's contextual vector more than a real edit does — so in the frozen-feature interface the banner is NOT 'trivially predictable/ignorable', it is a dominant nuisance (why finding 15a forced clean targets and status keeps flagging banner leak 0.45 pooled). BUT it does NOT cause the current negatives: gate 2 trains on CLEAN targets (banner stripped, gate2.py:283) and the codebook decoder strips nuisance entirely (finding 17), so the banner is bypassed in exactly the arms that fail. The gate failures are the copy-attractor (findings above), not the distractor. REFINES intuition (2): the banner is a legitimate and sharper-than-documented distractor for the REPRESENTATION/robustness question (Track A3, untested), but blaming it for the dynamics-gate negatives is misattribution.
findings.redesign: Keep the banner for the A3 state-equivalence-invariance test (a valid, quantified nuisance), but stop attributing dynamics-gate failures to it. For a slow-feature stress test a clean-target pipeline cannot bypass, the distractor must contaminate the SIGNAL-BEARING lines (e.g., per-file mutable metadata) rather than sit in a separable header, so a linear decoder cannot strip it.
findings.verify_note:
FINDING (refutes-hypothesis, SHARPENED): The per-trajectory banner is a valid but weaker-than-claimed slow-feature analog, and it does NOT cause the dynamics-gate negatives — those are the copy-attractor over a sparse target, not the distractor. Reference mechanism (2211.10831, JEPA.md:94): the trap needs a VARIANCE REGULARIZER that gets satisfied by copying a slow high-variance distractor; finding 12 confirms this bit the from-scratch slot recipe (25:1 SIGReg → banner 29.3%/19×, swap-ratio 3.22). It CANNOT bite the gate-2 arms because (i) they are evaluated on clean inputs and clean targets (run_round4.sh:12 / run_round5_genllm.sh:11 `--input-regime clean`; gate2.py:283,326), so the banner is doubly absent, and (ii) the gate-2 predictor is pure-L2 with no output variance term, so the 2211.10831 mechanism is structurally absent.

RECOMPUTES CONFIRMED (cache-val.pt, PYTHONHASHSEED=0): contamination (dirty vs clean) = 80.15 sum-L2 over 26.2 nonzero slots; state-changing signal (clean t+1 vs t) = 63.6; ratio 1.259, unit-invariant (SUM = per-slot-MEAN since both divide by 301). Copy-collapse confirmed: round-5 genllm state-changing h1 = 63.95 ≈ copy floor 63.13; symbolically only ~1.08/301 slots change per state-changing step (99.64% copy-identical) and the feature-space delta is 7.76% of target norm — copy is near-optimal, and 'trivially predictable' understates the banner but overstates the copy-attractor's dependence on it.

CORRECTIONS to the finding's numerology (do not change the verdict): (1) The '80 > 64, banner STRONGER than signal' claim conflates the slow banner with the FAST dynamic-noise line (render.py:15-21 adds both; noise resamples every step and is the OPPOSITE of a slow feature). Isolated: banner-only 57.2, noise-only 59.1, both 76→80. The BANNER ALONE (57.2) is BELOW signal (63.6), ratio 0.90 < 1; contamination exceeds signal only by including fast noise that is not the 2211.10831 trap. (2) 'perturbs every slot more than a real edit' is an aggregate-SUM artifact — per informative slot, signal concentrates (top-1 = 26.7, 41.5%) while contamination is diffuse haze (top-1 = 6.8, 8.9%); the banner is a low-amplitude smear, not a per-slot dominant nuisance. (3) The '14.6 signal slots vs 26.2 contam slots' comparison uses mismatched thresholds (>1.0 vs ~0; at a matched >1e-4, signal touches 27.3 > 26.2). (4) Cosmetic: '2 of 21 lines (9.5%)' → actual mean 29 lines, so 6.9%, consistent with the status doc's own '~3% of tokens' caveat (line 132).

CONCLUSION: The banner is a legitimate but weaker-than-documented distractor for the REPRESENTATION/robustness question (Track A3, untested for feature-space arms; already shown to bite variance-regularized from-scratch arms in finding 12). Blaming it for the dynamics-gate negatives is misattribution — a red herring for those specific failures. The load-bearing divergence for the negatives lies elsewhere (the sparse/near-copy target × frozen-feature interface), which is intuition (1)'s territory, not (2)'s.

data_redesign_proposals.name: Terminal-as-pixels: literal V-JEPA over rendered screenshots (computer-use regime)
data_redesign_proposals.description: Render each state/observation as an actual terminal SCREENSHOT (fixed-width monospace raster, e.g. 80x24 chars -> ~640x384 px, or a scrolling pane), and train a real V-JEPA / V-JEPA-2-AC stack: EMA-updated ViT target encoder + spatiotemporal multi-block masking, action-conditioned predictor over frozen or EMA patch tubelets. Keep the same underlying deterministic FS env and action set; only the observation channel changes from a text serialization to pixels. Optionally add a cursor, scrollback, and partial redraw so a command visibly repaints a screen region.
data_redesign_proposals.realism_for_agent: This is exactly what a computer-use / GUI agent actually perceives: pixels of a terminal emulator. It is the single most faithful analog to a deployed terminal agent and directly bridges to the V-JEPA-2 computer-use line of work. A real agent never receives a clean 301-slot symbolic map; it receives a rendered frame.
data_redesign_proposals.jepa_alignment: Restores every property JEPA needs at once: (1) DENSE/CONTINUOUS target - a command repaints many patches (new line pushes scrollback, cursor moves), so the masked-block target moves substantially every step; (2) HIGH-BANDWIDTH with genuine nuisance (font antialiasing, cursor blink, exact pixel positions) that an EMA target encoder must abstract away - this is precisely the regime JEPA.md 3.2 says reconstruction breaks and latent prediction wins, unlike our lossless text serialization where recon ties JEPA; (3) MASKED targets are structurally UN-COPYABLE (hidden from context encoder), eliminating the copy attractor that dominates 27.5% of our transitions and the 94% copy-fraction; (4) EMA/trained target encoder replaces frozen off-the-shelf ModernBERT, removing the non-canonical contextual-haze target. It moves us onto the exact recipe (2404.08471 / 2506.09985) the papers validated.
data_redesign_proposals.feasibility: Moderate-to-high effort. We already have the env (env/actions.py deterministic transition) and a renderer (env/render.py); we add a text->raster function (PIL monospace blit, deterministic, ~1 day) and swap the encoder/predictor for a V-JEPA ViT + tubelet masking + EMA target (this is a real training stack we do not currently have - the largest lift). datagen/generate.py trajectory loop is reusable unchanged. Risk: V-JEPA training is compute-heavier than our ModernBERT-feature regression; start with a small ViT-S and short clips.
data_redesign_proposals.expected_signal: The pre-registered gates flip their character: (a) reconstruction twin should NO LONGER tie JEPA (JEPA gains a latent-over-pixel edge, mirroring V-JEPA +5.1 K400), whereas our current recon twin matches/beats JEPA (finding 22). (b) Held-out-layout probe transfer should exceed random-init encoder (our current wall: random-init ties pretrained, recon content 0.117 < 0.175 floor). (c) Dynamics gate: masked action-conditioned prediction should beat copy on held-out layouts because copy is unavailable on masked patches - directly attacking the 63.95>63.13 collapse. If transfer STILL fails held-out here, that is a strong signal the wall is compositional binding independent of modality (see recon-twin binding evidence), which is itself a decisive result.

data_redesign_proposals.name: Verbose real-command-output rendering: state as a needle in high-entropy nuisance
data_redesign_proposals.description: Replace the canonical sorted [cK] tree with realistic, LOSSY, in-band command output: `ls -la` with permissions/owner/size/mtime timestamps and NON-sorted OS ordering; `cat` returning pseudo-random byte bodies whose CONTENT CLASS is a needle rather than a literal token; interleaved hostnames/PIDs/timestamps THROUGH the state lines (not a strippable header); occasional progress bars and log spew. The observation becomes partial+noisy: one command's stdout, not a full lossless serialization.
data_redesign_proposals.realism_for_agent: This is what a terminal agent actually reads: verbose, timestamped, unsorted, byte-level command output where the state-relevant bit is buried. It kills the two artifacts that make our env a toy - the globally-sorted line ordering (which lets a fixed line-key parser BE the readout, finding 14/22) and the class-token [cK] shortcut. A real agent must extract state from nuisance.
data_redesign_proposals.jepa_alignment: Directly targets the bandwidth-abstraction-regime divergence (JEPA.md 3.2). By injecting GENUINELY irreducible, high-entropy detail that CORRELATES with state (content-class -> a distribution over many-token bodies, sampled fresh), the target encoder finally has something to abstract: predict the semantic class, discard the surface bytes. This is where latent prediction is supposed to beat reconstruction. Non-strippable in-band nuisance also defeats the path-keyed-parser shortcut, forcing a learned canonical code rather than a line-key lookup. NOTE it does not by itself fix the copy-attractor unless paired with partial-obs (below).
data_redesign_proposals.feasibility: Low-to-moderate effort, fully within our stack. All changes are in env/render.py (rendering) and datagen/policies.py (which command to render); the symbolic env and probe ground-truth are untouched, so probing/gates keep working. Randomized ordering + byte bodies + interleaved metadata are ~2-3 days. We can keep the frozen-ModernBERT or upgrade to an EMA text encoder. Reuses evals/dynamics.py and probes wholesale.
data_redesign_proposals.expected_signal: (a) The reconstruction twin's parse-to-ceiling advantage collapses: content_given_exists should drop well below its current 1.000 path-keyed value because there is no [cK] token to read - if recon degrades while a JEPA/EMA arm holds, bandwidth is confirmed load-bearing for the JEPA-vs-recon DELTA. (b) Random-init encoder should NO LONGER tie pretrained (pretraining should now buy real abstraction of byte-level nuisance). (c) If held-out transfer still fails, it isolates the binding problem from the bandwidth problem - a clean scoped result either way.

data_redesign_proposals.name: Partial-obs stream + recurrent belief (W>1): dense action-observation coupling
data_redesign_proposals.description: Switch the primary regime from full-tree serialization to render_partial (env/render.py:43, already built): each observation is the prompt + stdout of the LAST command (ls one dir, cat one file, ~1.4 lines). Give the predictor a context window W in {3,5} or a GRU/recurrent state so it accumulates a belief over unobserved files. Goals become 'expected stdout of a diagnostic command' rather than a full rendered state.
data_redesign_proposals.realism_for_agent: This is how a terminal agent operates: it issues commands and sees only the local stdout of each, integrating a stream of partial views into a belief about the filesystem. Full-obs (seeing the entire tree every step) is unrealistic and was chosen only to isolate representation from memory (terminal-jepa.md 3).
data_redesign_proposals.jepa_alignment: Restores DENSE action-coupling: consecutive partial observations share 0.3% of lines vs 96.6% for full-obs (measured), so nearly every observation is fresh, action-driven signal and copy is no longer near-optimal - directly dissolving the 94% copy-fraction / 27.5% no-op copy attractor that collapses gate 2. Matches PLDM/V-JEPA-2-AC where each frame is action-determined and the copy baseline scores near-zero. CAVEAT (refutes 'free win'): it introduces partial observability, which the JEPA planning corpus assumes away (PLDM assumes full observability) - so it is a HARDER setting and must budget for memory, not just a render-flag flip.
data_redesign_proposals.feasibility: Moderate. render_partial exists; the real lift is architectural - the current predictor is W=1 with no belief state (models/nets.py:86; terminal-jepa.md 6: 'nothing maintains an explicit belief state'). Adding W>1 / GRU context is a real but bounded change to models/gate2.py + data loading (~3-5 days). Probe ground-truth still comes from the symbolic env, so gates are reusable.
data_redesign_proposals.expected_signal: Copy baseline stops being near-optimal (consecutive-obs copy drops from 0.975 to ~0.003), so a predictor that clears the dynamics gate MUST model dynamics. If a W>1 predictor beats copy on held-out layouts here while W=1 could not, memory was the missing ingredient. If it still fails, the failure is cleanly attributable to belief-tracking difficulty rather than target sparsity - separating the two deferred problems.

data_redesign_proposals.name: Correlated / cascading symbolic dynamics with macro-actions (staying symbolic but denser)
data_redesign_proposals.description: Keep the symbolic/oracle representation (which already CLEARS the gate: tier2 slotpred held-out state-changing 0.277 vs copy 1.076, AUC 0.906) but raise per-step target density and cross-slot structure: (a) macro-actions where one 'action' = a short script touching several correlated slots; (b) cascading dependencies (a write invalidates dependents; mkdir+touch chains; a rename propagates to referencing files); (c) content determined by a rule (content = hash(path) mod K, or a per-layout latent 'project type' dictating a joint content distribution) so masked-content prediction has real MI (currently I(content;visible)=0.010 bits, provably ill-posed for spatial A2). Drop the 27.5% no-op/invalid mass from the prediction loss.
data_redesign_proposals.realism_for_agent: Moderately realistic: real filesystems DO have correlated structure (config files reference each other, build artifacts depend on sources, project scaffolds imply joint content distributions). Real commands often cascade (rm -r, mv of a dir, a build step). Content is not i.i.d. uniform over 8 classes as our datagen makes it (layouts.py:32).
data_redesign_proposals.jepa_alignment: Two aligned effects. (1) Denser correlated dynamics make consecutive latent velocities acquire correlation (currently cos~0 random walk) and raise signal-fraction above the current 0.42-0.57, moving toward the dense regime. (2) Rule-based/correlated content restores the spatial-redundancy PRECONDITION that I-JEPA/Causal-JEPA masking exploits - masking a subtree becomes well-posed (I(masked;visible)>0), so a masked-prediction pretext can force a transferable code. Crucially this keeps the SYMBOLIC target where the gate is already clearable, sidestepping the frozen-contextual-feature wall entirely. It does NOT test JEPA's abstraction edge (no irreducible nuisance), so it is the 'make the world-model work' path, not the 'prove JEPA-vs-recon' path.
data_redesign_proposals.feasibility: Highest feasibility - lowest risk. All changes are in datagen (policies.py macro-actions/cascades, layouts.py correlated content) and the loss mask (gate2.py drop no-op mass). The winning tier2 symbolic pipeline already exists. ~2-4 days. No new training stack.
data_redesign_proposals.expected_signal: Symbolic dynamics prediction should extend from its current per-step 1-slot win to multi-slot correlated transitions while STAYING above copy on held-out layouts and improving compositional generalization (the real wall). A masked-content pretext over correlated content should for the first time beat the marginal-prior baseline (currently it cannot, I(content;visible)~0). If correlated content improves held-out binding, it directly attacks the identifiability wall that recon+JEPA both hit.

data_redesign_proposals.name: Real recorded agent sessions as the corpus (behavioral + observational realism)
data_redesign_proposals.description: Instead of synthetic policies (datagen/policies.py), record real (or LLM-agent-driven) terminal sessions against a sandboxed real filesystem: actual shell commands, actual stdout (ls/cat/grep/find/git), actual byte content, natural command distributions and error patterns. Store (observation_text_or_pixels, action, next_observation) triples. Derive probe ground-truth from the real FS state (snapshot the sandbox).
data_redesign_proposals.realism_for_agent: Maximal realism - this IS the target distribution. Command frequencies, output formats, error modes, file-content statistics, and multi-step task structure all match deployment. Removes every synthetic artifact (uniform i.i.d. content, sorted rendering, 64-banner distractor, fixed 301-slot universe, per-step [ts][pid] noise line).
data_redesign_proposals.jepa_alignment: Delivers the papers' data character: dense high-bandwidth observations with genuine irreducible nuisance (timestamps, hashes, ordering, byte content), natural distractors that are in-band and non-strippable, and non-uniform correlated structure that makes masking productive. It is the closest to 'a small amount of interaction data goes a long way' (JEPA.md 129) in the RIGHT regime. Pairs naturally with either the pixel or verbose-text encoder. Downside: no clean oracle symbolic control unless we snapshot FS state, and layout diversity/coverage becomes a data-collection problem rather than a datagen knob.
data_redesign_proposals.feasibility: Highest effort. Requires a sandboxed FS harness, session recording, an agent or scripted task suite to drive it, and a snapshot mechanism for ground-truth. Little of the current datagen reuses. Weeks, not days. Best pursued AFTER a cheaper redesign confirms the regime hypothesis, to avoid conflating data-engineering cost with a scientific negative.
data_redesign_proposals.expected_signal: If JEPA (pixel or verbose-text) trained on real sessions produces held-out probe transfer above random-init AND a dynamics predictor that beats copy on unseen filesystems, the synthetic-data-divergence hypothesis is confirmed end-to-end. If it STILL collapses to copy / fails transfer, that is the strongest possible evidence the domain itself is the wrong regime for JEPA - a decisive, publishable negative that no synthetic critique could establish.

hypothesis_verdicts.sparse_discrete_data: PARTLY TRUE BUT NOT THE LOAD-BEARING CAUSE (refined). The factual claim is confirmed exactly: a state-changing command moves mean 1.078 of 301 symbolic slots (92.7% exactly 1), 27.5% of all transitions are byte-identical no-ops/invalids with frozen-feature delta EXACTLY 0.0, and copy already achieves ~94% of target magnitude - a genuine, correctly-measured divergence from the dense/continuous V-JEPA/PLDM/DINO-WM regime where an action moves large patches and (for the masking papers) copy is structurally unavailable. This makes copy a strong competitor, makes the uniform sum-of-L2 metric forgiving of copy, and makes the no-op/invalid calibration clause the primary failing surface - so it is a real CONTRIBUTING amplifier. BUT it is decisively NOT the cause of the held-out negatives, proven by a clean dissociation on the project's OWN artifacts: under an ORACLE SYMBOLIC representation the IDENTICAL sparse dynamics (same 1.078-slot deltas, same 27.5% no-ops) are learned near-perfectly and TRANSFER to genuinely held-out layouts (tier2 slotpred: state-changing 0.277 vs copy 1.076 = 3.9x, no-op 0.000, AUC 0.906, VoE 0.952, 5 seeds, zero train/val layout overlap), whereas the SAME sparse dynamics over frozen ModernBERT features collapse to copy on held-out layouts (genllm 63.95 vs copy 63.13, AUC 0.803). Sparsity is identical in both arms; only the representation differs; only one fails. Moreover the frozen-feature target is NOT actually ~copy - ModernBERT contextualization smears a 1-slot symbolic edit across ~14.6 feature slots, so the failing arm's problem is synthesizing/transferring a dense contextual target, not a starved sparse signal. And the failing arm BEATS copy on SEEN layouts (copy-ratio 0.83-0.85); a gradient-starving copy attractor cannot coexist with beating copy in-distribution. Verdict: real modality mismatch, real amplifier, wrong root cause. Reducing no-op rate or up-weighting changed slots alone will NOT fix transfer (edit-weight 10 already fails).
hypothesis_verdicts.distractor_ill_conceived: NOT ILL-CONCEIVED, BUT LARGELY A RED HERRING FOR THE NEGATIVES UNDER REVIEW. The per-trajectory banner is a CORRECTLY-constructed slow-feature analog of arXiv 2211.10831: static within a trajectory (change-fraction 0.0000, the slowest channel), varying across trajectories (free variance a variance-regularizer can copy), 64 banners/6 bits. It provably BITES where the mechanism exists - the from-scratch slot encoder with a 25:1 variance hinge latches it at 19x chance (banner-swap 3.22) - a genuine text-domain replication of the paper's prediction. So the CONCEPT is valid, not ill-conceived. It is however (a) too LOW-BANDWIDTH to be load-bearing (1 line, ~3-9% of tokens, vs 2211.10831's full-frame distractor), and (b) mechanically NEUTRALIZED in exactly the arms that produce the negatives: gate-2 renders TARGETS clean (banner=None; feature delta on no-ops/invalids is exactly 0.0 verified), and there is no variance regularizer in the gate-2 loss - so the slow-feature copy mechanism cannot operate, and the banner literally cannot be the cause of the gate-2 collapse. The CLS recipe also suppresses it (swap 0.075) yet still fails held-out transfer, and the arm that leaks the banner MOST (frozen path-keyed) transfers perfectly - orthogonal to the wall. Two refinements: the dynamic-noise line is a FAST distractor (2211.10831's benign case), often conflated with the slow banner; and the genuinely damaging in-band nuisance is not the banner at all but ModernBERT's contextual bleed - which is deterministic and learnable, not an uncontrolled slow distractor, so it is not a 2211.10831 trap either. Verdict: keep the banner strictly as an RQ2/A3 robustness instrument; stop attributing any dynamics-gate or transfer negative to it. To make it a real trap you would need it dense, high-entropy, AND in the prediction target - which the clean-target design forbids by construction.
hypothesis_verdicts.wrong_regime_for_jepa: PARTIALLY THE WRONG REGIME, IN A SPECIFIC AND FIXABLE WAY - not a blanket disqualification. Two things are genuinely off-regime and confirmed. (1) The CLEAN full-obs observation is a LOSSLESS, deterministic, machine-readable serialization of the exact probe targets (parse is a tested inverse; content shown as [cK] tokens). This is precisely the regime JEPA.md 3.2 reserves NO edge for - 'text is discrete and low-bandwidth, so the unpredictable-detail problem is manageable' and reconstruction 'works well for text'. With zero irreducible detail to discard, JEPA's differentiating mechanism (a target encoder that throws away nuisance) is definitionally inert - which is exactly why the reconstruction twin ties/beats JEPA (recon macro 0.542 vs frozen JEPA 0.444; recon content 0.117 BELOW the 0.175 floor, i.e. its whole edge is the single cwd scalar) and why trained encoders decode held-out state WORSE than random-init. In this clean-serialization regime JEPA has nothing to do and cannot demonstrate value. (2) The smoothness/continuity planning priors (temporal-straightening, gradient planning, MPPI over smooth latents) are inapplicable to a discrete state space (consecutive latent velocity cos~0 even in a perfect one-hot world model - orthogonal by construction). BUT 'wrong regime' is NOT a blanket verdict, for three reasons: (a) world-modeling in representation space does not REQUIRE continuity - the discrete symbolic predictor clears the dynamics gate and transfers, so the portable JEPA core ('predict in representation space', JEPA.md 8) works here; discreteness is survivable by choosing a discrete predictor. (b) The action->next-state map is HIGH-MI and fully deterministic (a delta function), the OPPOSITE of a starved target - so the forward-dynamics objective is well-posed, matching V-JEPA-2-AC/PLDM which also predict mostly-persistent scenes. (c) The product-critical negative (cross-layout absolute-state transfer) is a COMPOSITIONAL BINDING failure that the recon twin shares and that survives any bandwidth fix - it is orthogonal to the regime question, not caused by it. So: the CLEAN-SERIALIZATION variant is the wrong regime (JEPA has no edge there and reconstruction is the honest baseline), but the DOMAIN is not inherently wrong - a redesign that injects real irreducible in-band nuisance (verbose outputs / pixels / partial-obs) moves it INTO JEPA's regime, and the discrete-symbolic path already gives a working world model. The blanket 'JEPA cannot work on terminals' claim is unsupported; the correct claim is 'JEPA has no edge on a lossless clean serialization, and continuity-exploiting planners are inapplicable to discrete state.'

load_bearing_divergences.title: Held-out-layout representation entanglement over the frozen contextual-feature interface (the actual wall; not sparsity, not the distractor)
load_bearing_divergences.why_load_bearing: This is where all 23 findings converge and it is proven by a clean dissociation on the project's own artifacts. Identical sparse discrete dynamics: under an oracle/symbolic representation the predictor CLEARS the gate and transfers to genuinely held-out layouts (tier2 slotpred state-changing 0.277 vs copy 1.076 = 3.9x, no-op 0.000, AUC 0.906, VoE 0.952, 5 seeds, zero layout overlap); over frozen ModernBERT features the SAME dynamics collapse to copy on held-out layouts (genllm 63.95 vs copy 63.13, AUC 0.803) while still beating copy on SEEN layouts (copy-ratio 0.83-0.85). Only the representation differs. The frozen per-line features are content-COMPLETE (linear decode reads content 100% held-out, even random-init) but not layout-CANONICAL - the learned dynamics memorize layout instances and cannot identify which abstract slot an action names on an unseen arrangement. This is the findings-3/10/11/13 identifiability problem resurfacing at the dynamics level. Sparsity, the banner, action-space size, and continuity are all orthogonal to it (each fails/succeeds identically on both sides of the dissociation).
load_bearing_divergences.which_findings: The dynamics-gate collapse (findings 21, 23), trained-encoders-decode-below-random (finding 13), recon-twin content below chance floor (finding 22), and every 'refutes-hypothesis' verdict in the battery.

load_bearing_divergences.title: Clean full-obs is a lossless deterministic serialization of the probe targets - zero irreducible detail for JEPA's target encoder to abstract (JEPA.md 3.2 low-bandwidth-text caveat)
load_bearing_divergences.why_load_bearing: Decisively explains the JEPA-vs-reconstruction PARITY and the trained-below-untrained damage: with a canonical, deterministic, [cK]-token tree the observation IS the answer in plain text, so a parser/reconstructor sits at ceiling (recon macro 0.542, its edge entirely the cwd scalar; content 0.117 < 0.175 floor) and a random-init encoder ties the pretrained one. JEPA's differentiating mechanism - a target encoder that discards nuisance - has nothing to buy, so abstraction can only DESTROY linearly-available symbols. This is the correct diagnosis of why JEPA shows no edge here, and it directly motivates the pixel / verbose-output redesigns. Load-bearing for the 'is this even JEPA's regime' question, though NOT the cause of the cross-layout transfer wall (which recon shares).
load_bearing_divergences.which_findings: Reconstruction-twin parity (finding 22), random-init ties pretrained (frozen-modernbert probe), the bandwidth-abstraction-regime findings.

load_bearing_divergences.title: Frozen off-the-shelf ModernBERT is a non-canonical target: bidirectional contextualization smears a 1-slot edit across ~14.6 feature slots, converting a sparse action-determined symbolic target into a dense, layout-idiosyncratic one
load_bearing_divergences.why_load_bearing: This is the mechanism that makes the frozen-feature interface (rank 1) fail where the symbolic interface succeeds. A single symbolic edit produces a target-delta that is only ~43-45% on the changed slot and ~55% diffuse contextual drift over ~26 textually-unchanged lines; the drift is deterministic but layout-dependent, so it does not transfer. It imposes a generative synthesis burden (VL-JEPA needed ~490M for this; our 0.6M-316M nets cannot carry it across layouts) and it is why no loss-weighting rescues the gate (edit-weight 0->10 monotonically worsens held-out). Load-bearing as the concrete cause of rank-1's failure, and it argues against ever regressing raw frozen contextual features - predict in a canonical symbolic/decoded space instead.
load_bearing_divergences.which_findings: Diffuse-haze diagnosis (finding 17), edit-weight sweeps (finding 21), generative-burden (finding 23), signal-vs-haze decompositions.

load_bearing_divergences.title: Discrete-sparse near-copy target + copy-available (anchor+delta, identity-at-init) predictor architecture makes copy the default attractor (CONTRIBUTING amplifier, not root cause)
load_bearing_divergences.why_load_bearing: A genuine modality mismatch vs dense/continuous video-robot data: mean 1.078/301 slots change, 27.5% exact no-ops (feature delta exactly 0.0), copy ~94% of target magnitude, and the predictor is identity-at-init over the full current state so copy is the initialization and the attractor - unlike the masking papers where the target is hidden and copy is structurally impossible. This makes the uniform sum-of-L2 metric forgiving of copy and makes the no-op/invalid calibration clause the primary failing surface. It AMPLIFIES the rank-1 failure and makes the gate numerically unforgiving, but the oracle-symbolic control learns the identical sparse dynamics fine, so it is not the load-bearing root. Ranked here because it is the most-cited intuition and a real, correctly-measured divergence that any redesign should fix (denser/masked/un-copyable targets).
load_bearing_divergences.which_findings: The change-sparsity, reference-fidelity copy-fraction, and observation-realism findings (all reclassified contributing).

load_bearing_divergences.title: Discreteness makes continuity-exploiting planners inapplicable, and the factored ~837k-arg action space starves CEM - both real, but downstream of gates 1-2 (planner-only)
load_bearing_divergences.why_load_bearing: Consecutive latent velocities are cos~0 (a random walk, even in a perfect one-hot world model) so temporal-straightening / gradient planning / MPPI-over-smooth-latents are structurally inapplicable; and the factored discrete action space (~837,081 arg combinations, needle P~1/865k) has no metric for CEM to sharpen (CEM +4 over random vs the +30 bar). Both are genuine reference-fidelity mismatches to every JEPA planner (all sample 2-7 dim continuous). But they bite ONLY at the planning gate, downstream of the representation/dynamics wall - the copy-collapse occurs under any action space, and sampling planning works fine in the cos~0 latent on the oracle (filtered 73%). Load-bearing for the END GOAL (planning) and for scoping the planner, NOT a cause of the current gate-1/2 negatives.
load_bearing_divergences.which_findings: Continuity-smoothness findings (demoted to red-herring for the encoder negatives), factored-action-space finding, planning findings 18/19.

synthesis.recommendation: SINGLE BEST NEXT DIRECTION: Move the observation channel INTO JEPA's regime while KEEPING the symbolic ground-truth for evaluation - specifically, run two cheap-to-moderate redesigns in parallel and let them cross-diagnose. PRIMARY (do first, lowest risk, highest information): 'verbose real-command-output rendering + partial-obs stream' (Proposals 2+3 combined). Render observations as realistic, LOSSY, in-band-noisy command output (unsorted ls -la with timestamps/permissions/sizes, cat returning pseudo-random bytes whose CLASS is a needle, interleaved PIDs/hostnames), and make each observation the stdout of the LAST command with a W>1 / recurrent predictor. This simultaneously (a) injects the irreducible nuisance JEPA's abstraction is FOR (fixing the rank-2 lossless-serialization divergence and the recon-twin parity), (b) kills the path-keyed-parser and [cK]-token shortcuts that make our env a toy, and (c) restores dense action-observation coupling (consecutive-obs copy 0.975->0.003), dissolving the rank-4 copy attractor. SECONDARY / DEFINITIVE (do second, once the above shows life): 'terminal-as-pixels literal V-JEPA' (Proposal 1) - the single most faithful analog to a real computer-use agent and the exact recipe (V-JEPA-2-AC) the papers validated, with EMA target encoder and structurally un-copyable masked targets that also fix the rank-3 non-canonical-frozen-ModernBERT interface. The critical experimental design point: in ALL of these, retain the ORACLE SYMBOLIC probe/gate and run the reconstruction twin as the honest baseline, because the load-bearing rank-1 wall (held-out-layout binding) is the one hypothesis these redesigns might NOT fix - if transfer still fails after moving fully into JEPA's regime, that isolates compositional binding as a modality-independent wall (a decisive, publishable result). WHAT TO KEEP: the deterministic symbolic env + action model (env/actions.py) as ground-truth; the tier2 SYMBOLIC predictor pipeline (it CLEARS the gate and transfers - this is the working world model, and the frozen-features -> codebook/symbol-grounding -> symbolic-dynamics route is the honest product path); the held-out-layout split and the 5-seed hardened battery; the banner ONLY as an A3/RQ2 robustness instrument. WHAT TO KILL: (1) regressing raw frozen off-the-shelf ModernBERT contextual features as the JEPA target - it is non-canonical and injects untransferable haze (rank 3); predict in a canonical symbolic/decoded space instead. (2) The clean full-obs lossless serialization as the PRIMARY regime - it is off-regime for JEPA and reconstruction is the honest baseline there; demote it to a parsing smoke test as pre-registered. (3) Any further effort strengthening the per-trajectory banner or attributing negatives to it, and any CEM +30-over-random planning bar in the full-obs combinatorial action space (Planner B / LLM-propose-world-model-rank is the only viable planner in that regime). (4) More no-op-rate / edit-weight tuning - it provably does not fix transfer. In one line: stop testing JEPA on a lossless clean serialization with a frozen non-canonical text encoder; put the observations into pixels/verbose-partial-obs where abstraction earns its keep, keep the symbolic oracle as ground-truth, and use the recon twin to tell you whether you have finally reached JEPA's regime - or hit the layout-binding wall that no modality change can fix."
